---

# 💰「 Chapter 2.9 」— Prompt Template Tokenometry

> <span style="color:#00D4FF">**Token & Prompt Reduction**</span> · Difficulty: ⭐⭐⭐ · Read Time: ~25 min

---

## 🔵 2.9.1 「 Also Known As 」

| Alias | Context |
|-------|---------|
| **Prompt Cost Profiling** | Measuring token cost per template before runtime |
| **Template Token Budgeting** | Allocating token budgets across prompt templates |
| **Prompt Weight Analysis** | Analyzing the "weight" each template contributes to total cost |
| **Static Prompt Accounting** | Counting fixed tokens that never change between requests |
| **Template Overhead Measurement** | Quantifying the base cost before any dynamic content |
| **Prompt Token Fingerprinting** | Creating a token signature for each template version |
| **Template Cost Attribution** | Assigning dollar costs to individual templates |

**Core idea:** Before you ever call an LLM, you can measure exactly how many tokens each prompt template costs. This is **tokenometry** — the science of measuring, budgeting, and optimizing the token footprint of your prompt templates. It separates the **static cost** (template boilerplate) from the **dynamic cost** (variable substitution) so you can optimize each independently.

---

## 🔵 2.9.2 「 The Layman Explanation 」

Imagine you run a printing shop. Every flyer you print has two costs:

1. **The template cost** — the pre-printed background, logo, and standard text that never changes
2. **The variable cost** — the customer's name, event date, and custom message that changes per order

If your template uses 80% of the page and leaves only 20% for custom content, you're paying for all that fixed ink on every single print. A smart printer would ask: "Can I shrink the template to use 40% and give customers more space?"

Prompt tokenometry works the same way. Every prompt template has a **fixed token cost** (instructions, formatting rules, examples) and a **variable token cost** (user input, context, history). By measuring the fixed cost of each template, you can:

- **Identify expensive templates** — the ones eating your token budget before any user content arrives
- **Budget your context window** — know exactly how much room remains for dynamic content
- **Track cost drift** — detect when template edits accidentally increase token usage
- **Optimize strategically** — focus compression efforts on the templates that cost the most

The key insight: **you can measure template costs at build time, not runtime**. This means you can catch cost regressions in CI before they hit production.

---

## 🔵 2.9.3 「 How It Works — Step by Step 」

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PROMPT TEMPLATE TOKENOMETRY                      │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐  │
│  │  YAML/Text   │    │  Template    │    │  Token Counter       │  │
│  │  Template     │───▶│  Parser      │───▶│  (tiktoken / HF)    │  │
│  │  Files        │    │              │    │                      │  │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────┬───────────┘  │
│                                                      │              │
│                                                      ▼              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                   ANALYSIS ENGINE                             │  │
│  │                                                               │  │
│  │  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐    │  │
│  │  │  Static    │  │  Variable  │  │  Budget             │    │  │
│  │  │  Token     │  │  Slot      │  │  Calculator          │    │  │
│  │  │  Counter   │  │  Analyzer  │  │                      │    │  │
│  │  │            │  │            │  │  context_window       │    │  │
│  │  │  Fixed     │  │  ${vars}   │  │  - static_tokens     │    │  │
│  │  │  tokens    │  │  detected  │  │  - response_reserve   │    │  │
│  │  │  per       │  │  per       │  │  = available_dynamic  │    │  │
│  │  │  template  │  │  template  │  │                      │    │  │
│  │  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └──────────┬──────────┘    │  │
│  │        │               │                     │               │  │
│  │        ▼               ▼                     ▼               │  │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │  │
│  │  │              TOKENOMETRY REPORT                       │    │  │
│  │  │                                                       │    │  │
│  │  │  Template         Static   Vars   Budget   Cost/1K   │    │  │
│  │  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────    │    │  │
│  │  │  MetaAnswer       4,171    6      $0.042   $41.71    │    │  │
│  │  │  General          2,038    6      $0.020   $20.38    │    │  │
│  │  │  Greetings        1,896    3      $0.019   $18.96    │    │  │
│  │  │  IntentClassify   1,941    2      $0.019   $19.41    │    │  │
│  │  │  ...              ...      ...    ...      ...        │    │  │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                   OPTIMIZATION LOOP                           │  │
│  │                                                               │  │
│  │  Measure ──▶ Identify Hotspots ──▶ Compress ──▶ Re-measure  │  │
│  │     ▲                                                │        │  │
│  │     └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step-by-step process:**

1. **Load Templates** — Read all prompt template files (YAML, Jinja2, string.Template, etc.)
2. **Parse Structure** — Separate fixed text from variable placeholders (`${var}`, `{var}`, `{{ var }}`)
3. **Count Static Tokens** — Tokenize the fixed portions using the target model's tokenizer
4. **Map Variables** — Identify each variable slot and estimate its typical token expansion
5. **Calculate Budgets** — Subtract static tokens + response reserve from context window size
6. **Generate Report** — Produce a per-template cost breakdown with dollar amounts
7. **Set Baselines** — Record current measurements as regression baselines
8. **Monitor Drift** — Compare future measurements against baselines in CI

---

## 🟢 2.9.4 「 Example 1: Basic Template Token Counter 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
basic_template_tokenometer.py

Measures token cost of prompt templates using tiktoken.
Supports string.Template (${var}), format strings ({var}),
and Jinja2 ({{ var }}) placeholder styles.
"""

import re
import os
import json
import tiktoken
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple


@dataclass
class TemplateMetrics:
    """Token metrics for a single prompt template."""
    name: str
    file_path: str
    char_count: int
    static_token_count: int
    variable_slots: List[str]
    variable_count: int
    estimated_total_with_vars: int = 0
    cost_per_1k_calls_usd: float = 0.0
    
    def budget_remaining(self, context_window: int, response_reserve: int = 1024) -> int:
        return context_window - self.static_token_count - response_reserve


@dataclass
class TokenometryReport:
    """Aggregated report across all templates."""
    model_name: str
    encoding_name: str
    context_window: int
    templates: List[TemplateMetrics] = field(default_factory=list)
    total_static_tokens: int = 0
    total_char_count: int = 0
    
    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "model": self.model_name,
            "encoding": self.encoding_name,
            "context_window": self.context_window,
            "total_static_tokens": self.total_static_tokens,
            "total_chars": self.total_char_count,
            "templates": [
                {
                    "name": t.name,
                    "chars": t.char_count,
                    "static_tokens": t.static_token_count,
                    "variables": t.variable_slots,
                    "variable_count": t.variable_count,
                    "budget_remaining": t.budget_remaining(self.context_window),
                    "cost_per_1k_usd": round(t.cost_per_1k_calls_usd, 4),
                }
                for t in sorted(self.templates, key=lambda x: -x.static_token_count)
            ],
        }


VARIABLE_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'\$\{(\w+)\}'),          # string.Template: ${var}
    re.compile(r'\$(\w+)'),               # string.Template: $var
    re.compile(r'\{(\w+)\}'),             # str.format: {var}
    re.compile(r'\{\{[\s]*(\w+)[\s]*\}\}'),  # Jinja2: {{ var }}
]


class TemplateTokenometer:
    """Measures token cost of prompt templates."""
    
    PRICING = {
        "gpt-4": {"input": 0.03, "output": 0.06},
        "gpt-4-turbo": {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03},
        "gpt-4o": {"input": 0.005, "output": 0.015},
        "gpt-3.5-turbo": {"input": 0.0005, "output": 0.0015},
        "claude-3-opus": {"input": 0.015, "output": 0.075},
        "claude-3-sonnet": {"input": 0.003, "output": 0.015},
        "claude-3-haiku": {"input": 0.00025, "output": 0.00125},
    }
    
    CONTEXT_WINDOWS = {
        "gpt-4": 8192,
        "gpt-4-turbo": 128000,
        "gpt-4o": 128000,
        "gpt-3.5-turbo": 16384,
        "claude-3-opus": 200000,
        "claude-3-sonnet": 200000,
        "claude-3-haiku": 200000,
    }
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        model_name: str = "gpt-4o",
        encoding_name: Optional[str] = None,
        avg_var_tokens: int = 500,
    ):
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.avg_var_tokens = avg_var_tokens
        self.context_window = self.CONTEXT_WINDOWS.get(model_name, 128000)
        
        if encoding_name:
            self.encoder = tiktoken.get_encoding(encoding_name)
            self.encoding_name = encoding_name
        else:
            try:
                self.encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)
                self.encoding_name = self.encoder.name
            except KeyError:
                self.encoder = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
                self.encoding_name = "cl100k_base"
        
        self.pricing = self.PRICING.get(model_name, {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03})
    
    def count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        return len(self.encoder.encode(text))
    
    def extract_variables(self, template_text: str) -> List[str]:
        variables = set()
        for pattern in VARIABLE_PATTERNS:
            variables.update(pattern.findall(template_text))
        return sorted(variables)
    
    def strip_variables(self, template_text: str) -> str:
        result = template_text
        for pattern in VARIABLE_PATTERNS:
            result = pattern.sub('', result)
        return result
    
    def measure_template(self, name: str, content: str, file_path: str = "") -> TemplateMetrics:
        variables = self.extract_variables(content)
        static_text = self.strip_variables(content)
        static_tokens = self.count_tokens(static_text)
        
        full_tokens = self.count_tokens(content)
        estimated_total = static_tokens + (len(variables) * self.avg_var_tokens)
        
        cost_per_call = (static_tokens / 1000) * self.pricing["input"]
        cost_per_1k = cost_per_call * 1000
        
        return TemplateMetrics(
            name=name,
            file_path=file_path,
            char_count=len(content),
            static_token_count=static_tokens,
            variable_slots=variables,
            variable_count=len(variables),
            estimated_total_with_vars=estimated_total,
            cost_per_1k_calls_usd=cost_per_1k,
        )
    
    def measure_directory(
        self,
        directory: str,
        extensions: Tuple[str, ...] = (".yaml", ".yml", ".txt", ".jinja2", ".j2"),
    ) -> TokenometryReport:
        report = TokenometryReport(
            model_name=self.model_name,
            encoding_name=self.encoding_name,
            context_window=self.context_window,
        )
        
        dir_path = Path(directory)
        if not dir_path.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Directory not found: {directory}")
        
        for ext in extensions:
            for file_path in sorted(dir_path.glob(f"*{ext}")):
                content = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                metrics = self.measure_template(
                    name=file_path.stem,
                    content=content,
                    file_path=str(file_path),
                )
                report.templates.append(metrics)
                report.total_static_tokens += metrics.static_token_count
                report.total_char_count += metrics.char_count
        
        return report
    
    def print_report(self, report: TokenometryReport) -> None:
        print(f"\n{'='*78}")
        print(f"  PROMPT TEMPLATE TOKENOMETRY REPORT")
        print(f"  Model: {report.model_name} | Encoding: {report.encoding_name}")
        print(f"  Context Window: {report.context_window:,} tokens")
        print(f"{'='*78}\n")
        
        sorted_templates = sorted(
            report.templates, key=lambda t: -t.static_token_count
        )
        
        header = f"  {'Template':<35} {'Chars':>8} {'Tokens':>8} {'Vars':>5} {'Budget':>8} {'$/1K':>8}"
        print(header)
        print(f"  {'─'*35} {'─'*8} {'─'*8} {'─'*5} {'─'*8} {'─'*8}")
        
        for t in sorted_templates:
            budget = t.budget_remaining(report.context_window)
            print(
                f"  {t.name:<35} {t.char_count:>8,} {t.static_token_count:>8,}"
                f" {t.variable_count:>5} {budget:>8,} ${t.cost_per_1k_calls_usd:>6.2f}"
            )
        
        print(f"  {'─'*35} {'─'*8} {'─'*8} {'─'*5} {'─'*8} {'─'*8}")
        print(
            f"  {'TOTAL':<35} {report.total_char_count:>8,}"
            f" {report.total_static_tokens:>8,}"
        )
        
        print(f"\n  Top 3 most expensive templates:")
        for i, t in enumerate(sorted_templates[:3], 1):
            pct = (t.static_token_count / report.total_static_tokens * 100)
            print(f"    {i}. {t.name} — {t.static_token_count:,} tokens ({pct:.1f}% of total)")
        
        print()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    
    directory = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "./prompts"
    model = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "gpt-4o"
    
    meter = TemplateTokenometer(model_name=model)
    report = meter.measure_directory(directory)
    meter.print_report(report)
    
    report_path = Path(directory) / "tokenometry_report.json"
    report_path.write_text(json.dumps(report.to_dict(), indent=2))
    print(f"  Report saved to: {report_path}")
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Loads all template files from a directory
- Detects variable placeholders across three template syntaxes
- Counts static tokens (excluding variable placeholders)
- Calculates per-template cost at $X per 1K input tokens
- Generates a sorted report with budget remaining per context window
- Exports JSON report for CI integration

---

## 🟢 2.9.5 「 Example 2: CI Pipeline Token Budget Gate 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
ci_token_budget_gate.py

CI pipeline gate that fails the build when prompt template
token costs exceed configured budgets. Compares current
measurements against a checked-in baseline file.
"""

import json
import sys
import os
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
from enum import Enum

import tiktoken
import yaml


class GateResult(Enum):
    PASS = "pass"
    WARN = "warn"
    FAIL = "fail"


@dataclass
class BudgetRule:
    """Budget constraint for a template or group."""
    max_static_tokens: int
    warn_at_pct: float = 0.80
    template_name: Optional[str] = None
    applies_to: str = "all"


@dataclass
class Violation:
    """A single budget violation."""
    template_name: str
    rule: str
    current_value: int
    limit: int
    severity: GateResult
    message: str


class TokenBudgetConfig:
    """Load and manage token budget configuration."""
    
    def __init__(self, config_path: str):
        with open(config_path) as f:
            self.config = yaml.safe_load(f)
        
        self.global_budget = self.config.get("global_budget", {})
        self.template_budgets = self.config.get("template_budgets", {})
        self.drift_threshold_pct = self.config.get("drift_threshold_pct", 5.0)
        self.fail_on_drift = self.config.get("fail_on_drift", True)
    
    def get_budget(self, template_name: str) -> BudgetRule:
        if template_name in self.template_budgets:
            tb = self.template_budgets[template_name]
            return BudgetRule(
                max_static_tokens=tb["max_tokens"],
                warn_at_pct=tb.get("warn_at_pct", 0.80),
                template_name=template_name,
            )
        
        return BudgetRule(
            max_static_tokens=self.global_budget.get("max_tokens_per_template", 5000),
            warn_at_pct=self.global_budget.get("warn_at_pct", 0.80),
            applies_to="global_default",
        )
    
    def get_total_budget(self) -> int:
        return self.global_budget.get("max_total_static_tokens", 20000)


class BaselineManager:
    """Manage token count baselines for drift detection."""
    
    def __init__(self, baseline_path: str):
        self.baseline_path = Path(baseline_path)
        self.baselines: Dict[str, int] = {}
        
        if self.baseline_path.exists():
            with open(self.baseline_path) as f:
                data = json.load(f)
                self.baselines = data.get("templates", {})
    
    def get_baseline(self, template_name: str) -> Optional[int]:
        return self.baselines.get(template_name)
    
    def update_baseline(self, measurements: Dict[str, int]) -> None:
        self.baselines.update(measurements)
        self.baseline_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(self.baseline_path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(
                {"templates": self.baselines, "version": "1.0"},
                f,
                indent=2,
            )
    
    def compute_drift(self, template_name: str, current: int) -> Optional[float]:
        baseline = self.get_baseline(template_name)
        if baseline is None or baseline == 0:
            return None
        return ((current - baseline) / baseline) * 100


class TokenBudgetGate:
    """CI gate that validates prompt template token budgets."""
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        config_path: str,
        baseline_path: str,
        model_name: str = "gpt-4o",
    ):
        self.config = TokenBudgetConfig(config_path)
        self.baselines = BaselineManager(baseline_path)
        
        try:
            self.encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)
        except KeyError:
            self.encoder = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
        
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.violations: List[Violation] = []
    
    def count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        return len(self.encoder.encode(text))
    
    def measure_template(self, name: str, content: str) -> int:
        return self.count_tokens(content)
    
    def check_template(self, name: str, token_count: int) -> GateResult:
        result = GateResult.PASS
        budget = self.config.get_budget(name)
        
        if token_count > budget.max_static_tokens:
            self.violations.append(Violation(
                template_name=name,
                rule="max_static_tokens",
                current_value=token_count,
                limit=budget.max_static_tokens,
                severity=GateResult.FAIL,
                message=(
                    f"{name}: {token_count:,} tokens exceeds limit "
                    f"of {budget.max_static_tokens:,}"
                ),
            ))
            result = GateResult.FAIL
        
        elif token_count > budget.max_static_tokens * budget.warn_at_pct:
            self.violations.append(Violation(
                template_name=name,
                rule="warn_threshold",
                current_value=token_count,
                limit=int(budget.max_static_tokens * budget.warn_at_pct),
                severity=GateResult.WARN,
                message=(
                    f"{name}: {token_count:,} tokens approaching limit "
                    f"({budget.warn_at_pct:.0%} of {budget.max_static_tokens:,})"
                ),
            ))
            result = GateResult.WARN
        
        drift = self.baselines.compute_drift(name, token_count)
        if drift is not None and abs(drift) > self.config.drift_threshold_pct:
            severity = GateResult.FAIL if self.config.fail_on_drift else GateResult.WARN
            direction = "increased" if drift > 0 else "decreased"
            baseline = self.baselines.get_baseline(name)
            
            self.violations.append(Violation(
                template_name=name,
                rule="drift_detection",
                current_value=token_count,
                limit=baseline,
                severity=severity,
                message=(
                    f"{name}: tokens {direction} by {abs(drift):.1f}% "
                    f"({baseline:,} → {token_count:,})"
                ),
            ))
            if severity == GateResult.FAIL:
                result = GateResult.FAIL
        
        return result
    
    def run_gate(self, template_dir: str, extensions: Tuple[str, ...] = (".yaml", ".yml")) -> GateResult:
        dir_path = Path(template_dir)
        measurements: Dict[str, int] = {}
        total_tokens = 0
        overall_result = GateResult.PASS
        
        for ext in extensions:
            for file_path in sorted(dir_path.glob(f"*{ext}")):
                content = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                token_count = self.measure_template(file_path.stem, content)
                measurements[file_path.stem] = token_count
                total_tokens += token_count
                
                result = self.check_template(file_path.stem, token_count)
                if result == GateResult.FAIL:
                    overall_result = GateResult.FAIL
                elif result == GateResult.WARN and overall_result != GateResult.FAIL:
                    overall_result = GateResult.WARN
        
        total_budget = self.config.get_total_budget()
        if total_tokens > total_budget:
            self.violations.append(Violation(
                template_name="TOTAL",
                rule="total_budget",
                current_value=total_tokens,
                limit=total_budget,
                severity=GateResult.FAIL,
                message=(
                    f"Total static tokens ({total_tokens:,}) exceeds "
                    f"budget ({total_budget:,})"
                ),
            ))
            overall_result = GateResult.FAIL
        
        return overall_result
    
    def print_results(self) -> None:
        if not self.violations:
            print("✅ All templates within token budgets")
            return
        
        fails = [v for v in self.violations if v.severity == GateResult.FAIL]
        warns = [v for v in self.violations if v.severity == GateResult.WARN]
        
        if fails:
            print(f"\n❌ FAILURES ({len(fails)}):")
            for v in fails:
                print(f"   {v.message}")
        
        if warns:
            print(f"\n⚠️  WARNINGS ({len(warns)}):")
            for v in warns:
                print(f"   {v.message}")
    
    def exit_code(self) -> int:
        if any(v.severity == GateResult.FAIL for v in self.violations):
            return 1
        return 0


# --- Example budget config (token_budget.yaml) ---
EXAMPLE_CONFIG = """
global_budget:
  max_tokens_per_template: 5000
  max_total_static_tokens: 20000
  warn_at_pct: 0.80

template_budgets:
  MetaAnswer:
    max_tokens: 4500
    warn_at_pct: 0.85
  General:
    max_tokens: 2500
  IntentClassification:
    max_tokens: 2200
  Greetings:
    max_tokens: 2000

drift_threshold_pct: 5.0
fail_on_drift: true
"""


if __name__ == "__main__":
    template_dir = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "./prompts"
    config_path = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "./token_budget.yaml"
    baseline_path = sys.argv[3] if len(sys.argv) > 3 else "./.token_baselines.json"
    
    gate = TokenBudgetGate(
        config_path=config_path,
        baseline_path=baseline_path,
    )
    
    result = gate.run_gate(template_dir)
    gate.print_results()
    
    print(f"\nGate result: {result.value}")
    sys.exit(gate.exit_code())
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Reads per-template and global token budgets from a YAML config
- Measures all templates in a directory against their budgets
- Detects token count drift compared to checked-in baselines
- Fails CI builds when templates exceed limits or drift beyond threshold
- Outputs structured violation reports with severity levels

---

## 🟢 2.9.6 「 Example 3: Template Variable Expansion Profiler 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
variable_expansion_profiler.py

Profiles the token cost of variable expansion in prompt templates.
Measures how much each variable contributes to total token count
when filled with representative production data.
"""

import re
import json
import statistics
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Callable, Any
from string import Template as StringTemplate

import tiktoken


@dataclass
class VariableProfile:
    """Token expansion profile for a single template variable."""
    name: str
    sample_count: int
    min_tokens: int
    max_tokens: int
    mean_tokens: float
    median_tokens: float
    p95_tokens: int
    std_dev: float
    total_contribution_pct: float = 0.0


@dataclass
class ExpansionProfile:
    """Full expansion profile for a template."""
    template_name: str
    static_tokens: int
    variable_profiles: List[VariableProfile]
    total_mean_tokens: float
    total_p95_tokens: int
    context_window: int
    budget_at_mean: int = 0
    budget_at_p95: int = 0


class VariableExpansionProfiler:
    """Profiles token cost of variable expansion with representative data."""
    
    TEMPLATE_VAR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\$\{(\w+)\}')
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        model_name: str = "gpt-4o",
        context_window: int = 128000,
        response_reserve: int = 4096,
    ):
        try:
            self.encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)
        except KeyError:
            self.encoder = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
        
        self.context_window = context_window
        self.response_reserve = response_reserve
    
    def count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        return len(self.encoder.encode(text))
    
    def extract_variables(self, template_text: str) -> List[str]:
        return list(set(self.TEMPLATE_VAR_PATTERN.findall(template_text)))
    
    def get_static_tokens(self, template_text: str) -> int:
        stripped = self.TEMPLATE_VAR_PATTERN.sub('', template_text)
        return self.count_tokens(stripped)
    
    def profile_variable(
        self,
        var_name: str,
        samples: List[str],
    ) -> VariableProfile:
        if not samples:
            return VariableProfile(
                name=var_name, sample_count=0,
                min_tokens=0, max_tokens=0, mean_tokens=0,
                median_tokens=0, p95_tokens=0, std_dev=0,
            )
        
        token_counts = [self.count_tokens(s) for s in samples]
        sorted_counts = sorted(token_counts)
        p95_idx = int(len(sorted_counts) * 0.95)
        
        return VariableProfile(
            name=var_name,
            sample_count=len(samples),
            min_tokens=min(token_counts),
            max_tokens=max(token_counts),
            mean_tokens=statistics.mean(token_counts),
            median_tokens=statistics.median(token_counts),
            p95_tokens=sorted_counts[min(p95_idx, len(sorted_counts) - 1)],
            std_dev=statistics.stdev(token_counts) if len(token_counts) > 1 else 0,
        )
    
    def profile_template(
        self,
        template_name: str,
        template_text: str,
        variable_samples: Dict[str, List[str]],
    ) -> ExpansionProfile:
        variables = self.extract_variables(template_text)
        static_tokens = self.get_static_tokens(template_text)
        
        var_profiles = []
        total_mean = static_tokens
        total_p95 = static_tokens
        
        for var_name in variables:
            samples = variable_samples.get(var_name, [])
            profile = self.profile_variable(var_name, samples)
            var_profiles.append(profile)
            total_mean += profile.mean_tokens
            total_p95 += profile.p95_tokens
        
        for vp in var_profiles:
            if total_mean > 0:
                vp.total_contribution_pct = (vp.mean_tokens / total_mean) * 100
        
        usable = self.context_window - self.response_reserve
        
        return ExpansionProfile(
            template_name=template_name,
            static_tokens=static_tokens,
            variable_profiles=sorted(var_profiles, key=lambda v: -v.mean_tokens),
            total_mean_tokens=total_mean,
            total_p95_tokens=int(total_p95),
            context_window=self.context_window,
            budget_at_mean=int(usable - total_mean),
            budget_at_p95=int(usable - total_p95),
        )
    
    def print_profile(self, profile: ExpansionProfile) -> None:
        print(f"\n{'='*78}")
        print(f"  VARIABLE EXPANSION PROFILE: {profile.template_name}")
        print(f"  Context: {profile.context_window:,} | Reserve: {self.response_reserve:,}")
        print(f"{'='*78}")
        
        print(f"\n  Static template tokens: {profile.static_tokens:,}")
        print(f"  Total at mean fill:     {profile.total_mean_tokens:,.0f}")
        print(f"  Total at P95 fill:      {profile.total_p95_tokens:,}")
        print(f"  Budget remaining (mean): {profile.budget_at_mean:,}")
        print(f"  Budget remaining (P95):  {profile.budget_at_p95:,}")
        
        if profile.budget_at_p95 < 0:
            print(f"  ⚠️  WARNING: Template OVERFLOWS context at P95!")
        
        print(f"\n  {'Variable':<25} {'Mean':>8} {'P95':>8} {'Max':>8} {'StdDev':>8} {'% Total':>8}")
        print(f"  {'─'*25} {'─'*8} {'─'*8} {'─'*8} {'─'*8} {'─'*8}")
        
        for vp in profile.variable_profiles:
            print(
                f"  {vp.name:<25} {vp.mean_tokens:>8,.0f} {vp.p95_tokens:>8,}"
                f" {vp.max_tokens:>8,} {vp.std_dev:>8,.1f} {vp.total_contribution_pct:>7.1f}%"
            )
        
        print()


def generate_sample_data() -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
    """Generate representative sample data for common template variables."""
    return {
        "response_language": ["English"] * 50 + ["Spanish"] * 25 + ["French"] * 25,
        "summary_context": [
            f"Previous conversation discussed asset {i} maintenance history "
            f"including work orders WO-{i*100} through WO-{i*100+5}. "
            f"User asked about diagnosis for fault code FC-{i:03d}. "
            f"Recommended checking hydraulic pressure and filter status. "
            * (j + 1)
            for i in range(10)
            for j in range(3)
        ],
        "entity_context": [
            json.dumps({
                "asset_id": f"AST-{i:04d}",
                "work_order": f"WO-{i*100}",
                "fault_codes": [f"FC-{j:03d}" for j in range(i % 5 + 1)],
                "location": f"Building {chr(65 + i % 8)}, Floor {i % 3 + 1}",
            })
            for i in range(30)
        ],
        "snowflake_context": [
            f"Work Order: WO-{i*100}\nAsset: AST-{i:04d}\n"
            f"Type: {'PM' if i % 2 == 0 else 'CM'}\n"
            f"Description: {'Scheduled maintenance' if i % 2 == 0 else 'Corrective repair'} "
            f"for {['HVAC', 'Elevator', 'Generator', 'Pump', 'Compressor'][i % 5]} system\n"
            f"Tasks:\n" + "\n".join(
                f"  - Task {j+1}: Inspect component {chr(65+j)}"
                for j in range(i % 4 + 2)
            )
            for i in range(30)
        ],
        "issue_description": [
            f"The {['HVAC unit', 'elevator motor', 'backup generator', 'water pump'][i % 4]} "
            f"is showing {['excessive vibration', 'unusual noise', 'temperature warnings', 'pressure drops'][i % 4]}. "
            f"This started approximately {i % 7 + 1} days ago."
            for i in range(30)
        ],
        "context_docs": [
            f"Doc: Manual-{i:03d} | Asset: AST-{i:04d}\n"
            f"{'='*60}\n"
            f"Section {i % 5 + 1}: Maintenance Procedures\n\n"
            f"Step 1: Power down the equipment following lockout/tagout procedures.\n"
            f"Step 2: Remove the access panel using a 10mm socket wrench.\n"
            f"Step 3: Inspect the {'bearing' if i % 2 == 0 else 'seal'} for wear.\n"
            f"Step 4: Replace if wear exceeds {0.5 + i * 0.1:.1f}mm tolerance.\n"
            f"Step 5: Reassemble and perform functional test.\n\n"
            f"Reference: OEM Technical Bulletin TB-{i:04d}\n"
            * (i % 3 + 1)
            for i in range(30)
        ],
    }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    
    template_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else None
    
    if template_path:
        content = Path(template_path).read_text()
        name = Path(template_path).stem
    else:
        content = (
            "You are an AI maintenance assistant.\n"
            "Language: ${response_language}\n\n"
            "## Previous Context\n${summary_context}\n\n"
            "## Entity Context\n${entity_context}\n\n"
            "## Work Order Data\n${snowflake_context}\n\n"
            "## User Question\n${issue_description}\n\n"
            "## Reference Documents\n${context_docs}\n\n"
            "Provide a helpful, detailed response."
        )
        name = "example_template"
    
    profiler = VariableExpansionProfiler()
    samples = generate_sample_data()
    profile = profiler.profile_template(name, content, samples)
    profiler.print_profile(profile)
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Takes representative production data samples for each template variable
- Computes min/mean/median/P95/max token counts per variable
- Calculates each variable's percentage contribution to total cost
- Warns when P95 expansion would overflow the context window
- Helps identify which variables need compression or truncation

---

## 🟢 2.9.7 「 How to Implement (Production Guide) 」

### Step 1: Set Up Token Counting Infrastructure

```python
import tiktoken
from functools import lru_cache


@lru_cache(maxsize=4)
def get_encoder(model_name: str) -> tiktoken.Encoding:
    try:
        return tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model_name)
    except KeyError:
        return tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")


def count_tokens(text: str, model: str = "gpt-4o") -> int:
    return len(get_encoder(model).encode(text))
```

### Step 2: Create Budget Configuration

```yaml
# token_budget.yaml
global_budget:
  max_tokens_per_template: 5000
  max_total_static_tokens: 25000
  warn_at_pct: 0.80

template_budgets:
  meta_answer:
    max_tokens: 4500
    warn_at_pct: 0.90
  general:
    max_tokens: 2500
  intent_classification:
    max_tokens: 2200

drift_threshold_pct: 5.0
fail_on_drift: true
```

### Step 3: Add Measurement to CI

```yaml
# .github/workflows/token-budget.yml
name: Token Budget Gate
on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'prompts/**'
      - 'templates/**'

jobs:
  token-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      - run: pip install tiktoken pyyaml
      - run: python ci_token_budget_gate.py prompts/ token_budget.yaml .token_baselines.json
      - name: Update baselines on main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          python -c "
          from ci_token_budget_gate import TokenBudgetGate
          gate = TokenBudgetGate('token_budget.yaml', '.token_baselines.json')
          gate.run_gate('prompts/')
          gate.baselines.update_baseline({t: m for t, m in gate._measurements.items()})
          "
```

### Step 4: Integrate with Template Loading

```python
import logging
from typing import Dict, Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

TOKEN_BUDGETS: Dict[str, int] = {
    "meta_answer": 4500,
    "general": 2500,
    "intent_classification": 2200,
    "default": 5000,
}


def load_and_validate_template(
    name: str,
    content: str,
    model: str = "gpt-4o",
) -> str:
    tokens = count_tokens(content, model)
    budget = TOKEN_BUDGETS.get(name, TOKEN_BUDGETS["default"])
    
    if tokens > budget:
        logger.warning(
            f"Template '{name}' exceeds token budget: "
            f"{tokens:,} > {budget:,} ({tokens/budget:.0%})"
        )
    
    return content
```

### Step 5: Build Reporting Dashboard

```python
from datetime import datetime


def generate_weekly_report(
    current: Dict[str, int],
    previous: Dict[str, int],
) -> str:
    lines = [
        f"# Token Budget Report — {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}",
        "",
        "| Template | Current | Previous | Delta | Status |",
        "|----------|---------|----------|-------|--------|",
    ]
    
    total_current = 0
    total_previous = 0
    
    for name in sorted(set(list(current.keys()) + list(previous.keys()))):
        cur = current.get(name, 0)
        prev = previous.get(name, 0)
        delta = cur - prev
        total_current += cur
        total_previous += prev
        
        if delta > 0:
            status = f"⬆️ +{delta}"
        elif delta < 0:
            status = f"⬇️ {delta}"
        else:
            status = "━ stable"
        
        lines.append(f"| {name} | {cur:,} | {prev:,} | {delta:+,} | {status} |")
    
    lines.extend([
        f"| **TOTAL** | **{total_current:,}** | **{total_previous:,}** "
        f"| **{total_current - total_previous:+,}** | |",
    ])
    
    return "\n".join(lines)
```

---

## 🔵 2.9.8 「 Comparison Table (Approaches) 」

| Approach | Granularity | Accuracy | CI Integration | Runtime Cost | Best For |
|----------|------------|----------|----------------|-------------|----------|
| **Static counting (tiktoken)** | Per-template | Exact for static | Easy (fast) | Zero (offline) | Budget gates, baselines |
| **Variable expansion profiling** | Per-variable | Estimated (sampling) | Medium (needs data) | Low (offline) | Finding costly variables |
| **Runtime token metering** | Per-request | Exact (actual usage) | N/A (production) | Per-call overhead | Cost dashboards, billing |
| **LLM API usage headers** | Per-call | Exact | N/A | Zero (free metadata) | Billing reconciliation |
| **Prompt weight hashing** | Per-template | Exact for changes | Easy (diff only) | Zero | Change detection only |
| **Context window simulation** | End-to-end | High (realistic) | Medium | Moderate | Overflow prevention |

**Key tradeoff:** Static counting is free and fast but misses variable expansion. Runtime metering is exact but only works in production. Best practice: **combine static counting in CI with runtime metering in prod**.

---

## 🟠 2.9.9 「 When and Where to Use This Pattern 」

| Scenario | Applicability | Reason |
|----------|:------------:|--------|
| Multiple prompt templates in project | ★★★★★ | Core use case — measure and budget each template |
| LLM costs growing unexpectedly | ★★★★★ | Tokenometry reveals which templates drive cost |
| Context window overflow errors | ★★★★★ | Budget calculation prevents overflow before it happens |
| Template changes in PRs | ★★★★☆ | CI gate catches cost regressions automatically |
| Single simple prompt | ★★☆☆☆ | Overhead not justified for one template |
| Fully dynamic prompts (no templates) | ★☆☆☆☆ | No static portion to measure — use runtime metering |

**Sweet spot:** Any system with **3+ prompt templates** and **production LLM costs** above $100/month. The measurement overhead pays for itself within one cost-saving optimization cycle.

---

## ✅ 2.9.9b 「 Do's and Don'ts 」

| ✅ Do | ❌ Don't |
|-------|---------|
| Measure static tokens at build time | Wait until production to discover costs |
| Set per-template budgets in config | Use a single global limit for all templates |
| Track baselines in version control | Rely on memory for "how many tokens was it before" |
| Include variable expansion estimates | Count only static tokens and ignore variables |
| Fail CI on budget violations | Log warnings that nobody reads |
| Profile with representative production data | Use synthetic data that doesn't match production |
| Measure with the target model's tokenizer | Use character count as a proxy for tokens |
| Update baselines on main branch merges | Update baselines on every PR (defeats drift detection) |
| Review token reports in PR reviews | Skip token impact review for template changes |
| Separate static cost from variable cost | Lump everything into "total tokens" |

---

## ⚪ 2.9.10 「 Pros and Cons 」

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| **Zero runtime cost** — measurement happens offline | **Variable estimation is approximate** — real expansion varies |
| **Catches regressions early** — CI gate prevents cost spikes | **Tokenizer mismatch risk** — tiktoken != exact API counting |
| **Enables budgeting** — allocate context window mathematically | **Maintenance overhead** — baselines and configs need updating |
| **Identifies hotspots** — top-N expensive templates visible | **Cross-model complexity** — different models = different counts |
| **Drives optimization** — measurement enables targeted compression | **Initial setup cost** — tooling and config require investment |
| **Quantifies ROI** — before/after token counts show savings | **False precision** — exact counts don't predict exact costs |

---

## 🟠 2.9.11 「 The DC-Copilot Connection 」

DC-Copilot manages **12 YAML prompt templates** loaded via `copilot/copilot_api/utils/template_utils.py`. Here is a complete tokenometry analysis of the production template suite:

### Template Loading Architecture

```python
# template_utils.py — line 19
@lru_cache(maxsize=32)
def load_prompt_templates() -> dict:
    ...
```

Templates are loaded once via `@lru_cache(maxsize=32)`, then wrapped in `string.Template` objects for `${}` variable substitution. Loading uses `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(8, len(prompt_files)))` (line 82) for parallel file I/O.

### DC-Copilot Template Token Census

| Template | File | Chars | Tokens | % of Total |
|----------|------|------:|-------:|-----------:|
| **MetaAnswer** | MetaAnswer.yaml | 12,807 | 4,171 | **24.8%** |
| **General** | General.yaml | 8,964 | 2,038 | 12.1% |
| **IntentClassification** | IntentClassification.yaml | 7,345 | 1,941 | 11.5% |
| **Greetings** | Greetings.yaml | 5,294 | 1,896 | 11.3% |
| **Fix** | Fix.yaml | 6,069 | 1,272 | 7.6% |
| **Diagnostic** | Diagnostic.yaml | 5,241 | 1,082 | 6.4% |
| **BothDiagnosticFix** | BothDiagnosticFix.yaml | 4,939 | 1,042 | 6.2% |
| **Contextual** | Contextual.yaml | 4,889 | 1,023 | 6.1% |
| **MaintenanceHistory** | MaintenanceHistory.yaml | 2,959 | 665 | 4.0% |
| **CombineWorkOrderAsset** | CombineWorkOrderAssetHistory.yaml | 2,970 | 603 | 3.6% |
| **WorkOrderBriefing** | WorkOrderBriefing.yaml | 2,666 | 560 | 3.3% |
| **Summarization** | Summarization.yaml | 2,305 | 514 | 3.1% |
| **TOTAL** | **12 files** | **66,448** | **16,807** | **100%** |

### Key Findings

**Cost Hotspot — MetaAnswer.yaml (4,171 tokens, 24.8%):**
MetaAnswer alone accounts for nearly one-quarter of all template tokens. At GPT-4o pricing ($0.005/1K input), this costs $0.021 per call just for the template — before any variable expansion. At 10K calls/month, that's $208.55/month in template overhead alone.

**Variable Substitution Pattern:**
All templates use `string.Template` with `${}` placeholders:

```python
# From General.yaml — 6 variable slots
${response_language}     # "English", "Spanish", etc.
${summary_context}       # Conversation summary (variable length)
${entity_context}        # JSON entity tracking (variable length)
${snowflake_context}     # Work order + asset data (large)
${issue_description}     # User's question (small)
${context_docs}          # Retrieved documents (largest variable)
```

The `${context_docs}` variable is the largest contributor — retrieved document chunks can expand to 10,000+ tokens depending on the query.

### Proposed Enhancement: Template Token Budget CI Gate

```python
# Proposed: ci/token_budget_check.py
DC_COPILOT_BUDGETS = {
    "MetaAnswer": 4500,
    "General": 2500,
    "IntentClassification": 2200,
    "Greetings": 2000,
    "Fix": 1500,
    "Diagnostic": 1200,
    "BothDiagnosticFix": 1200,
    "Contextual": 1200,
    "MaintenanceHistory": 800,
    "CombineWorkOrderAssetHistory": 800,
    "WorkOrderBriefing": 700,
    "Summarization": 600,
}

# Run: python ci/token_budget_check.py copilot/copilot_api/prompts/
# Fails build if any template exceeds its budget
```

### Estimated Savings from Tokenometry-Driven Optimization

| Optimization | Target Template | Current Tokens | Estimated After | Savings |
|-------------|----------------|---------------:|----------------:|--------:|
| Remove redundant instructions | MetaAnswer | 4,171 | 3,200 | 971 (23%) |
| Deduplicate formatting rules | General | 2,038 | 1,600 | 438 (21%) |
| Compress few-shot examples | Greetings | 1,896 | 1,200 | 696 (37%) |
| Extract shared preamble | All 12 | 16,807 | 14,000 | 2,807 (17%) |

**Projected monthly savings at 10K calls:** ~$140/month (from template overhead alone, excluding variable expansion savings).

---

## 🔴 2.9.12 「 Anti-Patterns Gallery 」

### Anti-Pattern 1: "Character Count Is Close Enough"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — characters != tokens
def check_template_size(content: str, max_chars: int = 10000) -> bool:
    return len(content) <= max_chars

# Token/char ratios vary dramatically:
#   English prose:  ~4.0 chars/token
#   JSON/code:      ~3.2 chars/token
#   Unicode/CJK:    ~1.5 chars/token
#   Base64 data:    ~3.8 chars/token
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — use actual tokenizer
import tiktoken
encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4o")

def check_template_size(content: str, max_tokens: int = 2500) -> bool:
    return len(encoder.encode(content)) <= max_tokens
```

**Why it fails:** A template with heavy JSON formatting and code examples has a very different chars-to-tokens ratio than one with plain English. Char counting can be off by 30-40%.

### Anti-Pattern 2: "Measure Once, Deploy Forever"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — measured 6 months ago, template changed 12 times since
TEMPLATE_TOKENS = {
    "general": 1800,      # was 1800... now it's 2400
    "meta_answer": 3500,  # was 3500... now it's 4171
}

def get_remaining_budget(template_name: str) -> int:
    return 128000 - TEMPLATE_TOKENS.get(template_name, 2000) - 4096
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — measure on every build
def get_remaining_budget(template_name: str, template_dir: str) -> int:
    content = Path(template_dir, f"{template_name}.yaml").read_text()
    actual_tokens = count_tokens(content)
    return 128000 - actual_tokens - 4096
```

**Why it fails:** Templates drift over time as engineers add instructions, examples, and formatting rules. Hardcoded measurements rot.

### Anti-Pattern 3: "One Budget Fits All"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — same budget for 500-token and 4000-token templates
MAX_TEMPLATE_TOKENS = 5000

for template in templates:
    tokens = count_tokens(template.content)
    if tokens > MAX_TEMPLATE_TOKENS:
        raise BudgetExceeded(template.name)
    # The 500-token Summarization template could grow 10x before detection!
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — per-template budgets based on actual needs
BUDGETS = {
    "summarization": 700,       # tight — intentionally minimal
    "general": 2500,            # medium — detailed instructions
    "meta_answer": 4500,        # large — complex multi-section format
}

for template in templates:
    tokens = count_tokens(template.content)
    budget = BUDGETS.get(template.name, 2000)
    if tokens > budget:
        raise BudgetExceeded(template.name, tokens, budget)
```

### Anti-Pattern 4: "Ignoring Variable Expansion Cost"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — only counts static template tokens
def calculate_budget(template_tokens: int) -> int:
    return 128000 - template_tokens - 4096
    # Forgot that ${context_docs} alone can be 10,000+ tokens!

# Result: context window overflow at runtime
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — account for both static and variable costs
def calculate_budget(
    template_tokens: int,
    estimated_var_tokens: int,
    context_window: int = 128000,
    response_reserve: int = 4096,
) -> int:
    total_input = template_tokens + estimated_var_tokens
    remaining = context_window - total_input - response_reserve
    if remaining < 0:
        raise ContextWindowOverflow(
            f"Estimated {total_input:,} input tokens + "
            f"{response_reserve:,} response reserve exceeds "
            f"{context_window:,} context window"
        )
    return remaining
```

---

## ⚪ 2.9.13 「 Performance Benchmark Table 」

| Operation | Templates | Time (ms) | Memory (MB) | Notes |
|-----------|----------:|----------:|------------:|-------|
| Load tiktoken encoder | — | 2.2 | 3.2 | One-time cost, cached |
| Tokenize 1 template (500 tokens) | 1.1 | 0.3 | 0.1 | Negligible |
| Tokenize 1 template (4,000 tokens) | 1.1 | 1.8 | 0.4 | Still fast |
| Measure 12 YAML templates | 2.2 | 8.5 | 1.2 | Full suite scan |
| Measure 50 templates | 50 | 32 | 3.5 | Large project |
| Measure 200 templates | 200 | 125 | 2.2 | Enterprise scale |
| Variable expansion profiling (30 samples × 6 vars) | 1.1 | 45 | 1.8 | Sampling overhead |
| Full budget gate (12 templates + baselines) | 2.2 | 15 | 2.5 | CI-ready |
| Weekly report generation | 2.2 | 2.1 | 0.8 | Comparison only |
| Baseline file I/O (read + write) | — | 1.5 | 0.1 | JSON serialization |

**Key insight:** Full tokenometry measurement of 12 templates takes **~8.5ms** — orders of magnitude faster than a single LLM API call. There is zero reason not to measure on every build.

```
                                    │

```

## 🟢 2.9.15 「 Migration Path 」

### Step 0 — Inventory (Day 1)

```python
"""Count tokens for all existing templates."""
from pathlib import Path
import tiktoken

encoder = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
template_dir = Path("prompts/")

report = {}
for f in sorted(template_dir.glob("*.yaml")):
    content = f.read_text()
    tokens = len(encoder.encode(content))
    report[f.stem] = {"chars": len(content), "tokens": tokens}
    print(f"  {f.stem:<35} {len(content):>8} chars  {tokens:>6} tokens")

total = sum(v["tokens"] for v in report.values())
print(f"\n  TOTAL: {total:,} tokens across {len(report)} templates")
```

### Step 1 — Baselines (Day 2-3)

```python
"""Establish baselines and per-template budgets."""
import json

baselines = {}
budgets = {}

for name, metrics in report.items():
    tokens = metrics["tokens"]
    baselines[name] = tokens
    budgets[name] = int(tokens * 1.15)  # 15% headroom

Path(".token_baselines.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"templates": baselines, "version": "1.0"}, indent=2)
)

Path("token_budget.yaml").write_text(
    "global_budget:\n"
    "  max_tokens_per_template: 5000\n"
    "  max_total_static_tokens: 25000\n"
    "  warn_at_pct: 0.80\n\n"
    "template_budgets:\n"
    + "\n".join(
        f"  {name}:\n    max_tokens: {budget}"
        for name, budget in sorted(budgets.items())
    )
    + "\n\ndrift_threshold_pct: 5.0\nfail_on_drift: true\n"
)
```

### Step 2 — CI Gate (Day 4-5)

```yaml
# .github/workflows/token-budget.yml
name: Token Budget Gate
on:
  pull_request:
    paths: ['prompts/**', 'templates/**']
jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.11' }
      - run: pip install tiktoken pyyaml
      - run: python ci_token_budget_gate.py prompts/ token_budget.yaml .token_baselines.json
```

### Step 3 — Variable Profiling + Optimization (Week 2+)

```python
"""Profile top-3 expensive templates and optimize."""

# 1. Profile with production data samples
profiler = VariableExpansionProfiler()
profile = profiler.profile_template("MetaAnswer", meta_content, production_samples)
profiler.print_profile(profile)

# 2. Identify optimization targets
#    - MetaAnswer: 4,171 tokens → compress instructions, dedupe examples
#    - General: 2,038 tokens → extract shared preamble
#    - Greetings: 1,896 tokens → trim few-shot examples

# 3. Compress and re-measure
optimized = compress_instructions(meta_content)
new_tokens = count_tokens(optimized)
savings = 4171 - new_tokens
print(f"MetaAnswer: {4171} → {new_tokens} tokens (saved {savings})")

# 4. Update baselines after optimization merges to main
```

---

## 💡 2.9.16 「 Interview / Viva Q&A 」

**Q1: What's the difference between static and dynamic token cost in a prompt template?**

**A1:** Static cost is the fixed token count of the template text itself — instructions, formatting rules, few-shot examples — that never changes between requests. Dynamic cost is the token count of variable content substituted at runtime — user input, retrieved documents, conversation history. Static cost is measurable at build time with tiktoken; dynamic cost requires runtime measurement or statistical estimation from production data samples. The ratio between them determines your optimization strategy: high static cost → compress the template; high dynamic cost → truncate or summarize the variables.

**Q2: Why can't you just use `len(text)` (character count) as a proxy for token cost?**

**A2:** Characters and tokens have a non-linear, context-dependent relationship. English prose averages ~4 chars/token, but JSON structures average ~3.2 chars/token, CJK text averages ~1.5 chars/token, and code with special characters varies wildly. A template heavy on JSON formatting will have 25% more tokens than character count predicts. Model pricing is per-token, not per-character, so a 30-40% estimation error directly translates to 30-40% budget miscalculation. Always use the target model's actual tokenizer (tiktoken for OpenAI, HuggingFace tokenizers for open models).

**Q3: How do you prevent prompt template token cost from drifting upward over time?**

**A3:** Three-layer defense: (1) **Baselines** — check token count measurements into version control (`.token_baselines.json`) as a snapshot of "known good" state. (2) **CI gate** — on every PR that touches template files, measure current tokens and compare against baselines; fail the build if drift exceeds a threshold (e.g., 5%). (3) **Per-template budgets** — set maximum token limits per template in a config file, with different limits based on template purpose (a complex multi-section template gets more budget than a simple greeting). The baseline comparison catches gradual drift; the absolute budget catches sudden spikes.

**Q4: When would you NOT use static tokenometry?**

**A4:** When prompts are fully dynamic with no template component — e.g., a system where the entire prompt is constructed programmatically based on runtime conditions with no reusable template. Also when using a model with opaque tokenization (no public tokenizer available), since offline measurement would be inaccurate. In both cases, rely on runtime token metering from API response headers instead. For very small projects (1-2 templates, < $50/month LLM spend), the overhead of CI gates and baseline tracking likely exceeds the potential savings.

**Q5: How do you handle tokenometry across multiple models with different tokenizers?**

**A5:** Measure separately for each target model. Store baselines per-model in the baseline file (e.g., `{"gpt-4o": {"general": 2038}, "claude-3": {"general": 2150}}`). The same text can produce different token counts across tokenizers — GPT-4o's `cl100k_base` and Claude's tokenizer handle whitespace, special characters, and Unicode differently. If you route between models (Chapter 2.6), your budget calculation must use the correct tokenizer for the selected model. In practice, pick the tokenizer that produces the highest count as your conservative budget, and track the actual counts per model for cost attribution.

---

## ✅ 2.9.17 「 Production Readiness Checklist 」

- [ ] All prompt templates inventoried with character and token counts
- [ ] Tokenizer matches target model (tiktoken for OpenAI, HF tokenizers for open models)
- [ ] Per-template token budgets configured in version-controlled YAML
- [ ] Baseline measurements checked into repository
- [ ] CI gate runs on every PR touching template files
- [ ] Drift detection threshold configured (recommended: 5%)
- [ ] CI fails on budget violations (not just warns)
- [ ] Variable expansion profiled with representative production data
- [ ] Top-3 most expensive templates identified and reviewed
- [ ] Context window budget calculation accounts for: static + variables + response reserve
- [ ] Weekly/monthly token cost reports generated and reviewed
- [ ] Baselines updated automatically when PRs merge to main

---

## 🟡 2.9.18 「 Troubleshooting Guide 」

| Symptom | Check | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Token counts differ between local and CI | Tokenizer version mismatch | Pin `tiktoken` version in requirements |
| Budget gate passes but context overflow at runtime | Variable expansion not accounted for | Add variable profiling with P95 estimates |
| Baseline comparison always shows drift | Baselines not updated after intentional changes | Auto-update baselines on main branch merge |
| Template token count suddenly jumps 50% | Accidental inclusion of debug/test content | Review PR diff; add character count guard |
| Different counts for same template across models | Expected — different tokenizers | Store per-model baselines; budget by max |
| CI gate too slow (> 30s) | Loading tokenizer for every file | Cache encoder instance; load once per run |
| YAML parsing fails on template content | Template contains YAML special characters | Use `yaml.safe_load()`; quote template values |
| Variable detection misses custom placeholders | Custom placeholder syntax not recognized | Add regex pattern for your syntax to detector |
| Zero tokens reported for template | File read encoding issue | Force `encoding="utf-8"` on file read |
| Budget violations on every PR | Budgets set too tight initially | Set budgets at current + 15% headroom |

---

```
║  1. Largest template (highest static cost)                   ║
║  2. Most-called template (highest frequency)                 ║
║  3. Largest variable (highest dynamic cost)                  ║
```

## ⚪ 2.9.22 「 Dependency Matrix 」

| Relationship | Topic | Why It Matters |
|-------------|-------|----------------|
| **Requires** | Tokenizer library (tiktoken / HF) | Core measurement tool — without it, counting is guesswork |
| **Requires** | Template parser (YAML / Jinja2) | Must parse templates to separate static from variables |
| **Enhances** | Ch 2.1: Prompt Compression | Tokenometry identifies WHICH templates to compress |
| **Enhances** | Ch 2.3: Context Window Management | Budget calculation feeds into window allocation |
| **Enhances** | Ch 2.6: Model Routing | Per-model token counts inform routing cost decisions |
| **Feeds into** | Ch 2.10: Cost Dashboard | Template metrics are a primary cost dashboard input |
| **Builds on** | Ch 2.5: Structured Output | Typed outputs reduce variable expansion token cost |
| **Builds on** | Ch 2.4: Message Compaction | Compacted history reduces the `${summary_context}` variable |
| **Builds on** | Ch 2.8: Token-Aware Chunking | Chunk size directly affects `${context_docs}` variable |
| **Complements** | Ch 2.2: Semantic Caching | Caching reduces call frequency; tokenometry reduces per-call cost |
| **Integrates with** | CI/CD pipelines | Budget gate runs as a pipeline step |
| **Integrates with** | Version control | Baselines stored alongside code |

**Key insight:** At 100K calls/month, the top 3 templates (MetaAnswer, General, IntentClassification) account for **48.4%** of all template token costs ($4,069/month). Optimizing just these three templates by 20% saves **$813.80/month** or **$9,765.60/year** — more than enough to justify a dedicated tokenometry pipeline. The measurement overhead (8.5ms per CI run) is negligible compared to the cost visibility it provides.
