# 💰「 Chapter 2.10 」— Cost Observability Dashboard Design

> <span style="color:#00D4FF">**Token & Prompt Reduction**</span> · Difficulty: ⭐⭐⭐ · Read Time: ~25 min

---

## 🔵 2.10.1 「 Also Known As 」

| Alias | Context |
|-------|---------|
| **LLM Cost Dashboard** | Visualizing real-time and historical LLM spending |
| **Token Spend Monitoring** | Tracking token consumption across endpoints and models |
| **AI Cost Observability** | Applying observability principles (metrics, logs, traces) to LLM costs |
| **Inference Cost Analytics** | Analyzing per-request and aggregate inference spending |
| **LLM FinOps** | Applying FinOps discipline to LLM infrastructure budgets |
| **Prompt Economics Dashboard** | Business-facing view of prompt cost vs. value delivered |
| **Model Spend Attribution** | Attributing LLM costs to teams, features, or tenants |

**Core idea:** You can't optimize what you can't see. A cost observability dashboard collects token usage metrics from every LLM call, aggregates them by endpoint, tenant, model, and time window, and presents actionable views — from real-time anomaly alerts to monthly budget tracking. It turns invisible API bills into engineering signals.

---

## 🔵 2.10.2 「 The Layman Explanation 」

Imagine running a restaurant where you serve hundreds of meals a day, but you have no idea which dishes cost the most to prepare, which tables order the most expensive items, or whether tonight's food cost is higher than last Tuesday's.

That's what running LLM-powered applications without cost observability looks like. Every API call consumes tokens — some cheap (a quick greeting), some expensive (a complex diagnosis with long context). Without a dashboard, you discover costs only when the monthly bill arrives.

A cost observability dashboard is your kitchen's real-time expense tracker:

- **Per-dish cost** → Per-endpoint token usage (chat vs. summary vs. document processing)
- **Per-table spending** → Per-tenant or per-session cost attribution
- **Daily food cost %** → Daily/weekly LLM spend trends
- **Waste tracking** → Failed requests that burned tokens but produced no value
- **Menu optimization** → Identifying which prompts to compress or cache

The dashboard doesn't reduce costs by itself — it tells you **where** costs come from so you can apply the right optimization (compression, caching, routing, batching) to the right place.

---

## 🔵 2.10.3 「 How It Works — Step by Step 」

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  COST OBSERVABILITY ARCHITECTURE                     │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐  │
│  │  LLM API     │    │  Metrics     │    │  Time-Series DB      │  │
│  │  Call         │───▶│  Collector   │───▶│  (InfluxDB /         │  │
│  │  (tokens,    │    │              │    │   Prometheus /        │  │
│  │   latency,   │    │  Extract:    │    │   CloudWatch)        │  │
│  │   model)     │    │  • tokens_in │    │                      │  │
│  └──────────────┘    │  • tokens_out│    └──────────┬───────────┘  │
│                      │  • model     │               │              │
│                      │  • endpoint  │               │              │
│                      │  • tenant_id │               ▼              │
│                      │  • latency   │    ┌──────────────────────┐  │
│                      └──────────────┘    │  Dashboard Engine    │  │
│                                          │  (Grafana / Streamlit│  │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    │   / Custom)          │  │
│  │  Structured  │    │  Log         │    │                      │  │
│  │  Logs        │───▶│  Aggregator  │───▶│  ┌────────────────┐ │  │
│  │  (JSON)      │    │  (ELK /      │    │  │ Real-Time View │ │  │
│  └──────────────┘    │   CloudWatch │    │  │ • Token/min    │ │  │
│                      │   Logs)      │    │  │ • Cost/hour    │ │  │
│                      └──────────────┘    │  │ • Error rate   │ │  │
│                                          │  └────────────────┘ │  │
│  ┌──────────────┐                        │  ┌────────────────┐ │  │
│  │  Budget      │                        │  │ Trend View     │ │  │
│  │  Alerts      │◀───────────────────────│  │ • Daily spend  │ │  │
│  │              │                        │  │ • Per-tenant   │ │  │
│  │  Slack /     │    Threshold           │  │ • Per-endpoint │ │  │
│  │  PagerDuty / │    exceeded            │  └────────────────┘ │  │
│  │  Email       │                        │  ┌────────────────┐ │  │
│  └──────────────┘                        │  │ Anomaly View   │ │  │
│                                          │  │ • Spikes       │ │  │
│                                          │  │ • Drift        │ │  │
│                                          │  │ • Outliers     │ │  │
│                                          │  └────────────────┘ │  │
│                                          └──────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step-by-step process:**

1. **Instrument** — Add token counting to every LLM call (input tokens, output tokens, model, latency)
2. **Enrich** — Attach metadata: endpoint, tenant_id, session_id, intent type, template name
3. **Emit** — Write structured metrics via logging or metrics SDK (Prometheus, StatsD, CloudWatch)
4. **Collect** — Aggregate metrics in a time-series database with retention policies
5. **Compute** — Calculate derived metrics: cost per call, cost per session, cost per tenant
6. **Visualize** — Build dashboard panels: real-time gauges, trend charts, top-N tables
7. **Alert** — Set budget thresholds with automated notifications on breach
8. **Act** — Use dashboard insights to prioritize optimization (Chapters 2.1-2.9)

---

## 🟢 2.10.4 「 Example 1: LLM Cost Metrics Collector 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
llm_cost_collector.py

Collects token usage metrics from every LLM call and emits
structured cost events for dashboard consumption.
"""

import time
import json
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from typing import Dict, Optional, Any, List
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum


class CostEventType(str, Enum):
    LLM_CALL = "llm_call"
    EMBEDDING_CALL = "embedding_call"
    BATCH_CALL = "batch_call"


MODEL_PRICING = {
    "gpt-4": {"input": 0.03, "output": 0.06},
    "gpt-4-turbo": {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03},
    "gpt-4o": {"input": 0.005, "output": 0.015},
    "gpt-4o-mini": {"input": 0.00015, "output": 0.0006},
    "gpt-3.5-turbo": {"input": 0.0005, "output": 0.0015},
    "claude-3-opus": {"input": 0.015, "output": 0.075},
    "claude-3-sonnet": {"input": 0.003, "output": 0.015},
    "claude-3-haiku": {"input": 0.00025, "output": 0.00125},
    "text-embedding-ada-002": {"input": 0.0001, "output": 0.0},
    "text-embedding-3-small": {"input": 0.00002, "output": 0.0},
}


@dataclass
class CostEvent:
    """A single LLM cost event with full attribution metadata."""
    timestamp: str
    event_type: str
    model: str
    input_tokens: int
    output_tokens: int
    total_tokens: int
    input_cost_usd: float
    output_cost_usd: float
    total_cost_usd: float
    latency_ms: float
    endpoint: str = ""
    tenant_id: str = ""
    session_id: str = ""
    intent: str = ""
    template_name: str = ""
    status: str = "success"
    error_message: str = ""
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def to_json(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps(asdict(self), default=str)

    def to_log_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "event": "llm_cost",
            **asdict(self),
        }


class LLMCostCollector:
    """Collects and emits LLM cost metrics."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
        pricing: Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, float]]] = None,
        default_model: str = "gpt-4o",
    ):
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("llm_cost_collector")
        self.pricing = pricing or MODEL_PRICING
        self.default_model = default_model
        self._events: List[CostEvent] = []
        self._session_totals: Dict[str, Dict[str, float]] = {}

    def calculate_cost(
        self,
        model: str,
        input_tokens: int,
        output_tokens: int,
    ) -> tuple:
        prices = self.pricing.get(model, self.pricing.get(self.default_model, {}))
        input_cost = (input_tokens / 1000) * prices.get("input", 0.01)
        output_cost = (output_tokens / 1000) * prices.get("output", 0.03)
        return input_cost, output_cost, input_cost + output_cost

    def record_call(
        self,
        model: str,
        input_tokens: int,
        output_tokens: int,
        latency_ms: float,
        endpoint: str = "",
        tenant_id: str = "",
        session_id: str = "",
        intent: str = "",
        template_name: str = "",
        status: str = "success",
        error_message: str = "",
        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> CostEvent:
        input_cost, output_cost, total_cost = self.calculate_cost(
            model, input_tokens, output_tokens
        )

        event = CostEvent(
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            event_type=CostEventType.LLM_CALL,
            model=model,
            input_tokens=input_tokens,
            output_tokens=output_tokens,
            total_tokens=input_tokens + output_tokens,
            input_cost_usd=round(input_cost, 6),
            output_cost_usd=round(output_cost, 6),
            total_cost_usd=round(total_cost, 6),
            latency_ms=round(latency_ms, 2),
            endpoint=endpoint,
            tenant_id=tenant_id,
            session_id=session_id,
            intent=intent,
            template_name=template_name,
            status=status,
            error_message=error_message,
            metadata=metadata or {},
        )

        self._events.append(event)
        self._update_session_totals(event)

        self.logger.info(
            "LLM_COST_EVENT",
            extra=event.to_log_dict(),
        )

        return event

    def _update_session_totals(self, event: CostEvent) -> None:
        key = event.session_id or "unknown"
        if key not in self._session_totals:
            self._session_totals[key] = {
                "total_cost_usd": 0.0,
                "total_input_tokens": 0,
                "total_output_tokens": 0,
                "call_count": 0,
            }
        totals = self._session_totals[key]
        totals["total_cost_usd"] += event.total_cost_usd
        totals["total_input_tokens"] += event.input_tokens
        totals["total_output_tokens"] += event.output_tokens
        totals["call_count"] += 1

    def get_session_totals(self, session_id: str) -> Dict[str, float]:
        return self._session_totals.get(session_id, {})

    def get_summary(self, window_minutes: int = 60) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp() - (window_minutes * 60)
        recent = [
            e for e in self._events
            if datetime.fromisoformat(e.timestamp).timestamp() > cutoff
        ]

        if not recent:
            return {"window_minutes": window_minutes, "total_calls": 0}

        total_cost = sum(e.total_cost_usd for e in recent)
        total_input = sum(e.input_tokens for e in recent)
        total_output = sum(e.output_tokens for e in recent)

        by_model = {}
        for e in recent:
            if e.model not in by_model:
                by_model[e.model] = {"calls": 0, "cost": 0.0, "tokens": 0}
            by_model[e.model]["calls"] += 1
            by_model[e.model]["cost"] += e.total_cost_usd
            by_model[e.model]["tokens"] += e.total_tokens

        by_endpoint = {}
        for e in recent:
            if e.endpoint not in by_endpoint:
                by_endpoint[e.endpoint] = {"calls": 0, "cost": 0.0}
            by_endpoint[e.endpoint]["calls"] += 1
            by_endpoint[e.endpoint]["cost"] += e.total_cost_usd

        by_tenant = {}
        for e in recent:
            if e.tenant_id not in by_tenant:
                by_tenant[e.tenant_id] = {"calls": 0, "cost": 0.0}
            by_tenant[e.tenant_id]["calls"] += 1
            by_tenant[e.tenant_id]["cost"] += e.total_cost_usd

        return {
            "window_minutes": window_minutes,
            "total_calls": len(recent),
            "total_cost_usd": round(total_cost, 4),
            "total_input_tokens": total_input,
            "total_output_tokens": total_output,
            "avg_cost_per_call": round(total_cost / len(recent), 6),
            "avg_latency_ms": round(
                sum(e.latency_ms for e in recent) / len(recent), 2
            ),
            "by_model": by_model,
            "by_endpoint": by_endpoint,
            "by_tenant": by_tenant,
            "error_count": sum(1 for e in recent if e.status != "success"),
            "error_cost_usd": round(
                sum(e.total_cost_usd for e in recent if e.status != "success"), 4
            ),
        }


class LLMCallTimer:
    """Context manager for timing LLM calls and recording cost events."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        collector: LLMCostCollector,
        model: str,
        endpoint: str = "",
        tenant_id: str = "",
        session_id: str = "",
        intent: str = "",
        template_name: str = "",
    ):
        self.collector = collector
        self.model = model
        self.endpoint = endpoint
        self.tenant_id = tenant_id
        self.session_id = session_id
        self.intent = intent
        self.template_name = template_name
        self._start_time = 0.0

    def __enter__(self):
        self._start_time = time.monotonic()
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        return False

    def record(
        self,
        input_tokens: int,
        output_tokens: int,
        status: str = "success",
        error_message: str = "",
    ) -> CostEvent:
        latency_ms = (time.monotonic() - self._start_time) * 1000
        return self.collector.record_call(
            model=self.model,
            input_tokens=input_tokens,
            output_tokens=output_tokens,
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            endpoint=self.endpoint,
            tenant_id=self.tenant_id,
            session_id=self.session_id,
            intent=self.intent,
            template_name=self.template_name,
            status=status,
            error_message=error_message,
        )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    collector = LLMCostCollector()

    with LLMCallTimer(
        collector, model="gpt-4o",
        endpoint="/chat", tenant_id="acme", session_id="sess-001",
        intent="diagnosis", template_name="Diagnostic",
    ) as timer:
        time.sleep(0.1)
        timer.record(input_tokens=2500, output_tokens=800)

    with LLMCallTimer(
        collector, model="gpt-4o",
        endpoint="/chat", tenant_id="acme", session_id="sess-001",
        intent="fix", template_name="Fix",
    ) as timer:
        time.sleep(0.05)
        timer.record(input_tokens=3200, output_tokens=1200)

    summary = collector.get_summary(window_minutes=5)
    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Records every LLM call with full attribution metadata (endpoint, tenant, intent, template)
- Calculates cost using per-model pricing tables
- Aggregates by session, model, endpoint, and tenant
- Provides a time-windowed summary for dashboard consumption
- Includes a context manager for automatic latency timing

---

## 🟢 2.10.5 「 Example 2: Grafana-Ready Prometheus Exporter 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
prometheus_cost_exporter.py

Exports LLM cost metrics in Prometheus format for Grafana dashboards.
Uses the prometheus_client library to expose /metrics endpoint.
"""

import time
import logging
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
from contextlib import contextmanager

from prometheus_client import (
    Counter,
    Histogram,
    Gauge,
    Summary,
    Info,
    CollectorRegistry,
    generate_latest,
    CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST,
)


REGISTRY = CollectorRegistry()

LLM_CALLS_TOTAL = Counter(
    "llm_calls_total",
    "Total number of LLM API calls",
    ["model", "endpoint", "tenant_id", "intent", "status"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_INPUT_TOKENS_TOTAL = Counter(
    "llm_input_tokens_total",
    "Total input tokens consumed",
    ["model", "endpoint", "tenant_id"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS_TOTAL = Counter(
    "llm_output_tokens_total",
    "Total output tokens generated",
    ["model", "endpoint", "tenant_id"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_COST_USD_TOTAL = Counter(
    "llm_cost_usd_total",
    "Total LLM cost in USD",
    ["model", "endpoint", "tenant_id"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_LATENCY_SECONDS = Histogram(
    "llm_latency_seconds",
    "LLM call latency in seconds",
    ["model", "endpoint"],
    buckets=[0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_TOKENS_PER_CALL = Histogram(
    "llm_tokens_per_call",
    "Token count distribution per call",
    ["model", "direction"],
    buckets=[100, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000, 50000, 100000],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_COST_PER_CALL_USD = Histogram(
    "llm_cost_per_call_usd",
    "Cost distribution per call in USD",
    ["model", "endpoint"],
    buckets=[0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_ACTIVE_REQUESTS = Gauge(
    "llm_active_requests",
    "Number of in-flight LLM requests",
    ["model", "endpoint"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_BUDGET_REMAINING_USD = Gauge(
    "llm_budget_remaining_usd",
    "Remaining budget in USD for the current period",
    ["period"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)

LLM_ERROR_COST_USD = Counter(
    "llm_error_cost_usd",
    "Cost of failed LLM calls (wasted spend)",
    ["model", "endpoint", "error_type"],
    registry=REGISTRY,
)


MODEL_PRICING = {
    "gpt-4o": {"input": 0.005, "output": 0.015},
    "gpt-4-turbo": {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03},
    "gpt-3.5-turbo": {"input": 0.0005, "output": 0.0015},
}


class PrometheusLLMExporter:
    """Exports LLM cost metrics for Prometheus/Grafana."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        pricing: Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, float]]] = None,
        monthly_budget_usd: float = 5000.0,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
    ):
        self.pricing = pricing or MODEL_PRICING
        self.monthly_budget_usd = monthly_budget_usd
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("prometheus_llm_exporter")
        self._total_spend = 0.0

        LLM_BUDGET_REMAINING_USD.labels(period="monthly").set(monthly_budget_usd)

    def calculate_cost(
        self, model: str, input_tokens: int, output_tokens: int
    ) -> float:
        prices = self.pricing.get(model, {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03})
        return (
            (input_tokens / 1000) * prices["input"]
            + (output_tokens / 1000) * prices["output"]
        )

    def record_call(
        self,
        model: str,
        input_tokens: int,
        output_tokens: int,
        latency_seconds: float,
        endpoint: str = "",
        tenant_id: str = "",
        intent: str = "",
        status: str = "success",
        error_type: str = "",
    ) -> float:
        cost = self.calculate_cost(model, input_tokens, output_tokens)

        LLM_CALLS_TOTAL.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint, tenant_id=tenant_id,
            intent=intent, status=status,
        ).inc()

        LLM_INPUT_TOKENS_TOTAL.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint, tenant_id=tenant_id,
        ).inc(input_tokens)

        LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS_TOTAL.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint, tenant_id=tenant_id,
        ).inc(output_tokens)

        LLM_COST_USD_TOTAL.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint, tenant_id=tenant_id,
        ).inc(cost)

        LLM_LATENCY_SECONDS.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint,
        ).observe(latency_seconds)

        LLM_TOKENS_PER_CALL.labels(model=model, direction="input").observe(input_tokens)
        LLM_TOKENS_PER_CALL.labels(model=model, direction="output").observe(output_tokens)

        LLM_COST_PER_CALL_USD.labels(
            model=model, endpoint=endpoint,
        ).observe(cost)

        if status != "success":
            LLM_ERROR_COST_USD.labels(
                model=model, endpoint=endpoint, error_type=error_type,
            ).inc(cost)

        self._total_spend += cost
        remaining = self.monthly_budget_usd - self._total_spend
        LLM_BUDGET_REMAINING_USD.labels(period="monthly").set(max(0, remaining))

        return cost

    @contextmanager
    def track_call(
        self,
        model: str,
        endpoint: str = "",
        tenant_id: str = "",
        intent: str = "",
    ):
        LLM_ACTIVE_REQUESTS.labels(model=model, endpoint=endpoint).inc()
        start = time.monotonic()

        class CallTracker:
            def __init__(self, exporter):
                self._exporter = exporter
                self.cost = 0.0

            def finish(
                self,
                input_tokens: int,
                output_tokens: int,
                status: str = "success",
                error_type: str = "",
            ):
                latency = time.monotonic() - start
                self.cost = self._exporter.record_call(
                    model=model,
                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
                    latency_seconds=latency,
                    endpoint=endpoint,
                    tenant_id=tenant_id,
                    intent=intent,
                    status=status,
                    error_type=error_type,
                )

        tracker = CallTracker(self)
        try:
            yield tracker
        finally:
            LLM_ACTIVE_REQUESTS.labels(model=model, endpoint=endpoint).dec()

    def get_metrics(self) -> bytes:
        return generate_latest(REGISTRY)

    def get_content_type(self) -> str:
        return CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST


def create_fastapi_metrics_endpoint(app, exporter: PrometheusLLMExporter):
    """Add /metrics endpoint to a FastAPI app."""
    from fastapi import Response

    @app.get("/metrics", tags=["Monitoring"])
    async def metrics():
        return Response(
            content=exporter.get_metrics(),
            media_type=exporter.get_content_type(),
        )


GRAFANA_DASHBOARD_JSON = {
    "dashboard": {
        "title": "LLM Cost Observability",
        "panels": [
            {
                "title": "Total Cost (24h)",
                "type": "stat",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'sum(increase(llm_cost_usd_total[24h]))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Cost by Model",
                "type": "piechart",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'sum by (model)(increase(llm_cost_usd_total[24h]))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Cost by Endpoint",
                "type": "barchart",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'sum by (endpoint)(increase(llm_cost_usd_total[1h]))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Cost per Call (P95)",
                "type": "timeseries",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(llm_cost_per_call_usd_bucket[5m]))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Token Throughput",
                "type": "timeseries",
                "targets": [
                    {"expr": 'sum(rate(llm_input_tokens_total[5m]))', "legendFormat": "Input tokens/s"},
                    {"expr": 'sum(rate(llm_output_tokens_total[5m]))', "legendFormat": "Output tokens/s"},
                ],
            },
            {
                "title": "Budget Remaining",
                "type": "gauge",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'llm_budget_remaining_usd{period="monthly"}'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Error Cost (Wasted Spend)",
                "type": "stat",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'sum(increase(llm_error_cost_usd[24h]))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Latency Distribution",
                "type": "heatmap",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'rate(llm_latency_seconds_bucket[5m])'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Top Tenants by Cost",
                "type": "table",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'topk(10, sum by (tenant_id)(increase(llm_cost_usd_total[24h])))'}],
            },
            {
                "title": "Active Requests",
                "type": "timeseries",
                "targets": [{"expr": 'sum(llm_active_requests)'}],
            },
        ],
    }
}


if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    exporter = PrometheusLLMExporter(monthly_budget_usd=5000.0)

    with exporter.track_call("gpt-4o", endpoint="/chat", tenant_id="acme") as t:
        time.sleep(0.1)
        t.finish(input_tokens=3000, output_tokens=1000)

    with exporter.track_call("gpt-4o", endpoint="/summary", tenant_id="acme") as t:
        time.sleep(0.05)
        t.finish(input_tokens=5000, output_tokens=500, status="error", error_type="timeout")

    print(exporter.get_metrics().decode())
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Exports Prometheus counters, histograms, and gauges for all LLM cost dimensions
- Tracks input/output tokens, cost in USD, latency, and error waste separately
- Provides a context manager (`track_call`) for automatic latency + active-request tracking
- Includes a Grafana dashboard JSON template with 10 pre-built panels
- Manages a monthly budget gauge that decrements with each call

## 🟢 2.10.6 「 Example 3: Budget Alert System with Anomaly Detection 」

<details>
<summary>📝 Click to expand full production code</summary>

```python
"""
budget_alert_system.py

Monitors LLM spending against budgets and detects cost anomalies.
Sends alerts via configurable channels (Slack, email, PagerDuty).
"""

import json
import logging
import statistics
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Callable, Any
from enum import Enum
from collections import deque


class AlertSeverity(str, Enum):
    INFO = "info"
    WARNING = "warning"
    CRITICAL = "critical"


class AlertChannel(str, Enum):
    LOG = "log"
    SLACK = "slack"
    EMAIL = "email"
    PAGERDUTY = "pagerduty"


@dataclass
class BudgetConfig:
    """Budget configuration for a specific scope."""
    scope: str
    daily_budget_usd: float
    monthly_budget_usd: float
    warn_at_pct: float = 0.75
    critical_at_pct: float = 0.90
    anomaly_std_devs: float = 2.5
    max_cost_per_call_usd: float = 1.0
    max_calls_per_hour: int = 1000


@dataclass
class CostAlert:
    """A cost alert event."""
    timestamp: str
    severity: AlertSeverity
    scope: str
    rule: str
    message: str
    current_value: float
    threshold: float
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def to_slack_block(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        emoji = {"info": "ℹ️", "warning": "⚠️", "critical": "🚨"}[self.severity]
        return {
            "blocks": [
                {
                    "type": "header",
                    "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": f"{emoji} LLM Cost Alert"},
                },
                {
                    "type": "section",
                    "fields": [
                        {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Severity:* {self.severity.value}"},
                        {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Scope:* {self.scope}"},
                        {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Rule:* {self.rule}"},
                        {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Current:* ${self.current_value:.4f}"},
                        {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Threshold:* ${self.threshold:.4f}"},
                    ],
                },
                {
                    "type": "section",
                    "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": self.message},
                },
            ]
        }


class CostAnomalyDetector:
    """Detects cost anomalies using statistical methods."""

    def __init__(self, window_size: int = 168, min_samples: int = 24):
        self.window_size = window_size
        self.min_samples = min_samples
        self._hourly_costs: deque = deque(maxlen=window_size)
        self._hourly_calls: deque = deque(maxlen=window_size)

    def add_observation(self, hourly_cost: float, hourly_calls: int) -> None:
        self._hourly_costs.append(hourly_cost)
        self._hourly_calls.append(hourly_calls)

    def is_cost_anomaly(self, current_cost: float, std_devs: float = 2.5) -> Optional[Dict]:
        if len(self._hourly_costs) < self.min_samples:
            return None

        mean = statistics.mean(self._hourly_costs)
        stdev = statistics.stdev(self._hourly_costs)

        if stdev == 0:
            return None

        z_score = (current_cost - mean) / stdev

        if abs(z_score) > std_devs:
            return {
                "z_score": round(z_score, 2),
                "mean": round(mean, 4),
                "stdev": round(stdev, 4),
                "threshold": round(mean + std_devs * stdev, 4),
                "direction": "spike" if z_score > 0 else "drop",
            }

        return None

    def is_call_volume_anomaly(
        self, current_calls: int, std_devs: float = 3.0
    ) -> Optional[Dict]:
        if len(self._hourly_calls) < self.min_samples:
            return None

        mean = statistics.mean(self._hourly_calls)
        stdev = statistics.stdev(self._hourly_calls)

        if stdev == 0:
            return None

        z_score = (current_calls - mean) / stdev

        if abs(z_score) > std_devs:
            return {
                "z_score": round(z_score, 2),
                "mean": round(mean, 1),
                "stdev": round(stdev, 1),
                "direction": "spike" if z_score > 0 else "drop",
            }

        return None


class BudgetAlertSystem:
    """Monitors LLM spending and fires alerts on budget breaches."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        budgets: Dict[str, BudgetConfig],
        alert_handlers: Optional[Dict[AlertChannel, Callable]] = None,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
        cooldown_minutes: int = 30,
    ):
        self.budgets = budgets
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("budget_alerts")
        self.cooldown_minutes = cooldown_minutes
        self._alert_handlers = alert_handlers or {}
        self._daily_spend: Dict[str, float] = {}
        self._monthly_spend: Dict[str, float] = {}
        self._hourly_calls: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._last_alert: Dict[str, datetime] = {}
        self._anomaly_detectors: Dict[str, CostAnomalyDetector] = {}
        self._alerts_fired: List[CostAlert] = []

        for scope in budgets:
            self._anomaly_detectors[scope] = CostAnomalyDetector()

    def record_cost(
        self,
        scope: str,
        cost_usd: float,
        call_count: int = 1,
    ) -> List[CostAlert]:
        self._daily_spend[scope] = self._daily_spend.get(scope, 0) + cost_usd
        self._monthly_spend[scope] = self._monthly_spend.get(scope, 0) + cost_usd
        self._hourly_calls[scope] = self._hourly_calls.get(scope, 0) + call_count

        alerts = []
        config = self.budgets.get(scope)
        if not config:
            return alerts

        daily = self._daily_spend.get(scope, 0)
        if daily > config.daily_budget_usd * config.critical_at_pct:
            alert = self._create_alert(
                scope, AlertSeverity.CRITICAL, "daily_budget_critical",
                f"Daily spend ${daily:.2f} exceeds {config.critical_at_pct:.0%} "
                f"of ${config.daily_budget_usd:.2f} budget",
                daily, config.daily_budget_usd * config.critical_at_pct,
            )
            if alert:
                alerts.append(alert)
        elif daily > config.daily_budget_usd * config.warn_at_pct:
            alert = self._create_alert(
                scope, AlertSeverity.WARNING, "daily_budget_warning",
                f"Daily spend ${daily:.2f} exceeds {config.warn_at_pct:.0%} "
                f"of ${config.daily_budget_usd:.2f} budget",
                daily, config.daily_budget_usd * config.warn_at_pct,
            )
            if alert:
                alerts.append(alert)

        if cost_usd > config.max_cost_per_call_usd:
            alert = self._create_alert(
                scope, AlertSeverity.WARNING, "expensive_call",
                f"Single call cost ${cost_usd:.4f} exceeds "
                f"${config.max_cost_per_call_usd:.2f} threshold",
                cost_usd, config.max_cost_per_call_usd,
            )
            if alert:
                alerts.append(alert)

        detector = self._anomaly_detectors.get(scope)
        if detector:
            anomaly = detector.is_cost_anomaly(
                cost_usd, config.anomaly_std_devs
            )
            if anomaly:
                alert = self._create_alert(
                    scope, AlertSeverity.WARNING, "cost_anomaly",
                    f"Cost anomaly detected: ${cost_usd:.4f} is "
                    f"{anomaly['z_score']:.1f} std devs from mean "
                    f"(${anomaly['mean']:.4f} ± ${anomaly['stdev']:.4f})",
                    cost_usd, anomaly["threshold"],
                    metadata=anomaly,
                )
                if alert:
                    alerts.append(alert)

        for alert in alerts:
            self._dispatch_alert(alert)

        return alerts

    def _create_alert(
        self,
        scope: str,
        severity: AlertSeverity,
        rule: str,
        message: str,
        current_value: float,
        threshold: float,
        metadata: Optional[Dict] = None,
    ) -> Optional[CostAlert]:
        alert_key = f"{scope}:{rule}"
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        if alert_key in self._last_alert:
            elapsed = (now - self._last_alert[alert_key]).total_seconds() / 60
            if elapsed < self.cooldown_minutes:
                return None

        self._last_alert[alert_key] = now

        alert = CostAlert(
            timestamp=now.isoformat(),
            severity=severity,
            scope=scope,
            rule=rule,
            message=message,
            current_value=current_value,
            threshold=threshold,
            metadata=metadata or {},
        )
        self._alerts_fired.append(alert)
        return alert

    def _dispatch_alert(self, alert: CostAlert) -> None:
        self.logger.log(
            logging.CRITICAL if alert.severity == AlertSeverity.CRITICAL
            else logging.WARNING,
            f"COST_ALERT [{alert.severity.value}] {alert.scope}: {alert.message}",
        )

        for channel, handler in self._alert_handlers.items():
            try:
                handler(alert)
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.error(f"Failed to dispatch alert to {channel}: {e}")

    def reset_daily(self) -> None:
        self._daily_spend.clear()
        self._hourly_calls.clear()

    def reset_monthly(self) -> None:
        self._monthly_spend.clear()

    def get_budget_status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        status = {}
        for scope, config in self.budgets.items():
            daily = self._daily_spend.get(scope, 0)
            monthly = self._monthly_spend.get(scope, 0)
            status[scope] = {
                "daily_spend": round(daily, 4),
                "daily_budget": config.daily_budget_usd,
                "daily_pct": round((daily / config.daily_budget_usd) * 100, 1)
                if config.daily_budget_usd > 0 else 0,
                "monthly_spend": round(monthly, 4),
                "monthly_budget": config.monthly_budget_usd,
                "monthly_pct": round((monthly / config.monthly_budget_usd) * 100, 1)
                if config.monthly_budget_usd > 0 else 0,
                "alerts_fired": len([
                    a for a in self._alerts_fired if a.scope == scope
                ]),
            }
        return status


if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

    budgets = {
        "production": BudgetConfig(
            scope="production",
            daily_budget_usd=200.0,
            monthly_budget_usd=5000.0,
            warn_at_pct=0.75,
            critical_at_pct=0.90,
        ),
        "staging": BudgetConfig(
            scope="staging",
            daily_budget_usd=50.0,
            monthly_budget_usd=500.0,
        ),
    }

    system = BudgetAlertSystem(budgets=budgets)

    for i in range(20):
        cost = 8.5 + (i * 0.5)
        alerts = system.record_cost("production", cost)
        for a in alerts:
            print(f"  ALERT: [{a.severity.value}] {a.message}")

    print("\nBudget Status:")
    print(json.dumps(system.get_budget_status(), indent=2))
```

</details>

**What this does:**
- Manages per-scope budget configs (daily + monthly limits, warn/critical thresholds)
- Detects cost anomalies using z-score statistics over sliding hourly windows
- Fires alerts with cooldown to prevent alert storms
- Supports multiple dispatch channels (Slack, email, PagerDuty)
- Tracks wasted spend from failed calls separately

---

## 🟢 2.10.7 「 How to Implement (Production Guide) 」

### Step 1: Instrument LLM Calls

```python
import time
from typing import Generator


def instrumented_llm_stream(
    llm_service,
    prompt: str,
    cost_collector: "LLMCostCollector",
    model: str = "gpt-4o",
    endpoint: str = "/chat",
    tenant_id: str = "",
    session_id: str = "",
) -> Generator[str, None, None]:
    start = time.monotonic()
    input_tokens = len(prompt) // 4  # rough estimate pre-call
    output_tokens = 0
    status = "success"
    error_msg = ""

    try:
        for token in llm_service.stream(prompt):
            output_tokens += 1
            yield token
    except Exception as e:
        status = "error"
        error_msg = str(e)
        raise
    finally:
        latency_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000
        cost_collector.record_call(
            model=model,
            input_tokens=input_tokens,
            output_tokens=output_tokens,
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            endpoint=endpoint,
            tenant_id=tenant_id,
            session_id=session_id,
            status=status,
            error_message=error_msg,
        )
```

### Step 2: Add Structured Cost Logging

```python
import json
import logging


class CostStructuredLogger:
    """Emits cost events as structured JSON log lines."""

    def __init__(self, logger: logging.Logger):
        self.logger = logger

    def log_cost_event(self, event: dict) -> None:
        self.logger.info(json.dumps({
            "event_type": "llm_cost",
            "timestamp": event["timestamp"],
            "model": event["model"],
            "input_tokens": event["input_tokens"],
            "output_tokens": event["output_tokens"],
            "cost_usd": event["total_cost_usd"],
            "latency_ms": event["latency_ms"],
            "endpoint": event["endpoint"],
            "tenant_id": event["tenant_id"],
            "session_id": event["session_id"],
            "intent": event.get("intent", ""),
            "status": event["status"],
        }))
```

### Step 3: Set Up Metrics Pipeline

```yaml

version: '3.8'
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:v2.48.0
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:10.2.0
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin
    volumes:
      - ./grafana/dashboards:/var/lib/grafana/dashboards
```

```yaml
# prometheus.yml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'copilot-api'
    scrape_interval: 15s
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['host.docker.internal:8000']
    metrics_path: '/metrics'
```

### Step 4: Configure Budget Alerts

```python
import os

BUDGET_CONFIGS = {
    "production": BudgetConfig(
        scope="production",
        daily_budget_usd=float(os.getenv("LLM_DAILY_BUDGET", "200")),
        monthly_budget_usd=float(os.getenv("LLM_MONTHLY_BUDGET", "5000")),
        warn_at_pct=0.75,
        critical_at_pct=0.90,
        max_cost_per_call_usd=1.0,
    ),
}


def send_slack_alert(alert: "CostAlert") -> None:
    import requests
    webhook_url = os.getenv("SLACK_COST_WEBHOOK")
    if webhook_url:
        requests.post(webhook_url, json=alert.to_slack_block(), timeout=5)
```

## 🔵 2.10.8 「 Comparison Table (Approaches) 」

| Approach | Setup Cost | Granularity | Real-Time | Cost | Best For |
|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|------|----------|
| **Structured logging + ELK** | Low | Per-call | Near (seconds) | Low (self-hosted) | Small-medium teams with ELK |
| **Prometheus + Grafana** | Medium | Per-call | Yes (15s scrape) | Low (self-hosted) | Engineering-heavy dashboards |
| **CloudWatch metrics** | Low | Per-call | Yes (1 min) | Medium (AWS pricing) | AWS-native deployments |
| **Datadog APM** | Low (SaaS) | Per-call | Yes | High ($$$) | Enterprise with Datadog |
| **Custom Streamlit dashboard** | Medium | Aggregated | Near (polling) | Low | Quick internal tools |
| **LangSmith** | Low (SaaS) | Per-trace | Yes | Medium | LangChain/LangGraph apps |
| **OpenTelemetry + Jaeger** | High | Per-span | Yes | Low (self-hosted) | Microservices with tracing |
| **API provider dashboard** | Zero | Per-day | No (daily) | Free | Basic billing only |

**Key tradeoff:** Self-hosted (Prometheus/Grafana) gives maximum control and low cost but requires maintenance. SaaS tools (Datadog, LangSmith) are easier but expensive at scale. For most teams, **structured logging + Prometheus/Grafana** is the sweet spot.

---

## 🟠 2.10.9 「 When and Where to Use This Pattern 」

| Scenario | Applicability | Reason |
|----------|:------------:|--------|
| LLM spend > $500/month | ★★★★★ | Dashboard pays for itself in optimization insights |
| Multi-tenant application | ★★★★★ | Must attribute costs to tenants for billing/fairness |
| Multiple LLM endpoints | ★★★★★ | Need per-endpoint cost breakdown |
| Budget-constrained project | ★★★★☆ | Prevents surprise overruns |
| Single-developer side project | ★★☆☆☆ | API provider dashboard sufficient |
| Fixed-price contract (no per-token billing) | ★☆☆☆☆ | Token costs hidden by flat rate |

**Sweet spot:** Any production system with **multiple endpoints, multiple tenants, or monthly LLM spend exceeding $500**. At that scale, even a 10% optimization identified by the dashboard saves more than the setup cost.

---

## ✅ 2.10.9b 「 Do's and Don'ts 」

| ✅ Do | ❌ Don't |
|-------|---------|
| Track input and output tokens separately | Lump all tokens into "total" |
| Attribute costs to tenants/endpoints/intents | Track only aggregate spend |
| Set alerts at 75% and 90% of budget | Wait for 100% to find out |
| Track failed-call costs as "waste" | Ignore the cost of errors |
| Include latency alongside cost | Optimize cost without watching latency |
| Dashboard for 3 audiences (exec, eng, ops) | Build one dashboard for everyone |
| Use cooldowns on alerts (30+ min) | Fire alerts on every threshold cross |
| Store raw events for drill-down analysis | Store only aggregates (lose detail) |
| Measure at the LLM call boundary | Measure at the HTTP endpoint (misses retries) |
| Version your dashboard configs | Rebuild dashboards from memory |

---

## ⚪ 2.10.10 「 Pros and Cons 」

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| **Visibility** — see where every dollar goes | **Setup investment** — instrumentation + infra takes days |
| **Budget control** — alerts prevent overruns | **Maintenance burden** — dashboards need updating as system evolves |
| **Optimization targeting** — data shows what to optimize first | **Metric explosion** — high-cardinality labels can overwhelm Prometheus |
| **Tenant attribution** — enables per-tenant billing | **Approximate token counts** — pre-call estimates != exact API counts |
| **Anomaly detection** — catches runaway costs early | **Alert fatigue** — poorly tuned thresholds generate noise |
| **Historical analysis** — trends reveal patterns | **Storage cost** — raw event storage grows with traffic |
| **Compliance** — audit trail of LLM usage | **Cold start** — anomaly detection needs history before it works |

---

## 🟠 2.10.11 「 The DC-Copilot Connection 」

DC-Copilot currently has **no cost observability dashboard** — LLM costs are invisible until the Azure OpenAI monthly invoice arrives. Here's how one could be built using the existing infrastructure.

### Current State: What Exists

**Logging infrastructure** (`common/util/logging.py`):

```python
# Singleton Logger — console + file handlers
logger = Logger(
    name='copilot-proactive-guidance',
    level=logging.INFO,
    log_to_file=True
).get_logger()
```

The `Logger` class (line 6) provides a centralized singleton with console + file handlers. All nodes already use `self.logger.info(...)` and `self.logger.error(...)`. The format is: `%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s`.

**API endpoints** (`api/api.py`):
- `/chat` (line 154) — SSE streaming endpoint, main LLM consumer
- `/chatInitialize` (line 252) — Pre-computes context, no direct LLM calls
- `/summary` (line 400) — Auto-summarization, single LLM call per request
- `/document` (line 332) — Document processing, embedding calls only

**LLM call sites:**
- `LLMService.invoke()` — Non-streaming, 3-attempt retry (used by `/summary`)
- `LLMService.stream()` — Streaming, 3-attempt retry (used by `/chat`)
- Both use `MAX_PROMPT_LENGTH = 320000` chars truncation

### Proposed Dashboard Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                DC-COPILOT COST DASHBOARD                 │
│                                                          │
│  ┌──────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ LLMService       │    │ CloudWatch Metrics          │ │
│  │ .invoke()        │───▶│                             │ │
│  │ .stream()        │    │ • llm_input_tokens          │ │
│  │                  │    │ • llm_output_tokens          │ │
│  │ Add after each   │    │ • llm_cost_usd              │ │
│  │ successful call: │    │ • llm_latency_ms            │ │
│  │ logger.info(     │    │ • llm_retry_count           │ │
│  │   json.dumps({   │    │                             │ │
│  │     tokens_in,   │    │ Dimensions:                 │ │
│  │     tokens_out,  │    │ • endpoint (/chat, /summary)│ │
│  │     cost_usd,    │    │ • tenant_id                 │ │
│  │     ...          │    │ • intent                    │ │
│  │   })             │    │ • template_name             │ │
│  │ )                │    │ • model                     │ │
│  └──────────────────┘    └─────────────┬──────────────┘ │
│                                         │                │
│                                         ▼                │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │          GRAFANA DASHBOARD PANELS                 │   │
│  │                                                   │   │
│  │  ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ │   │
│  │  │ Daily     │ │ Cost by   │ │ Top Tenants    │ │   │
│  │  │ Spend     │ │ Endpoint  │ │ by Spend       │ │   │
│  │  │ $142.50   │ │ /chat 87% │ │ 1. acme $42   │ │   │
│  │  │           │ │ /summ 13% │ │ 2. globex $38  │ │   │
│  │  └───────────┘ └───────────┘ └────────────────┘ │   │
│  │  ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ │   │

│  │  │ Remaining │ │ Waste     │ │ Breakdown      │ │   │
```

### Implementation Path for DC-Copilot

**Step 1 — Add cost logging to LLMService (2 lines per method):**

```python
# In LLMService.invoke() — after successful response:
self.logger.info(json.dumps({
    "event": "llm_cost", "endpoint": "invoke",
    "input_tokens": len(prompt) // 4,  # approximate
    "output_tokens": len(response) // 4,
    "model": "gpt-4o", "tenant_id": tenant_id,
}))

# In LLMService.stream() — after stream completes:
self.logger.info(json.dumps({
    "event": "llm_cost", "endpoint": "stream",
    "input_tokens": len(prompt) // 4,
    "output_tokens": token_count,
    "model": "gpt-4o", "tenant_id": tenant_id,
}))
```

**Step 2 — CloudWatch Metrics Filter** (extracts from existing logs):

```json
{
  "filterPattern": "{ $.event = \"llm_cost\" }",
  "metricTransformations": [
    {
      "metricName": "LLMInputTokens",
      "metricNamespace": "DCCopilot/LLM",
      "metricValue": "$.input_tokens",
      "dimensions": { "Endpoint": "$.endpoint", "TenantId": "$.tenant_id" }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Step 3 — Estimated Cost per Endpoint (current DC-Copilot):**

| Endpoint | Template Tokens | Avg Variable Tokens | Total/Call | Cost/Call (GPT-4o) |
|----------|----------------:|--------------------:|-----------:|-------------------:|
| `/chat` (diagnosis) | 1,082 | ~8,000 | ~9,082 | $0.045 + output |
| `/chat` (general) | 2,038 | ~6,000 | ~8,038 | $0.040 + output |
| `/chat` (meta_answer) | 4,171 | ~4,000 | ~8,171 | $0.041 + output |
| `/summary` | 514 | ~3,000 | ~3,514 | $0.018 + output |
| `/document` (embed) | 0 | ~250/chunk | ~250 | $0.000005/chunk |

**At 10K chat calls + 1K summary calls/month:** ~$530 estimated (input only, excluding output tokens and embeddings).

## 🔴 2.10.12 「 Anti-Patterns Gallery 」

### Anti-Pattern 1: "Track Only Totals"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — one number tells you nothing actionable
daily_total_cost = sum(call.cost for call in today_calls)
logger.info(f"Today's LLM cost: ${daily_total_cost:.2f}")

# "We spent $150 today" — but on what? Which tenant? Which endpoint?
# Can't optimize without attribution.
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — multi-dimensional attribution
for call in today_calls:
    logger.info(json.dumps({
        "cost_usd": call.cost,
        "endpoint": call.endpoint,
        "tenant_id": call.tenant_id,
        "intent": call.intent,
        "model": call.model,
        "template": call.template_name,
    }))

# Now you know: /chat costs 87%, tenant "acme" costs 35%,
# MetaAnswer template is 25% of template overhead
```

### Anti-Pattern 2: "Alert on Every Violation"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — alert storm during traffic spike
def check_budget(cost: float, budget: float) -> None:
    if cost > budget * 0.75:
        send_slack_alert(f"Budget warning: ${cost:.2f}")
    # Fires 200 alerts in an hour during peak traffic
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — cooldown + deduplication
class AlertManager:
    def __init__(self, cooldown_minutes: int = 30):
        self.cooldown = cooldown_minutes
        self._last_alert = {}

    def maybe_alert(self, rule: str, cost: float, budget: float) -> None:
        now = datetime.now()
        if rule in self._last_alert:
            elapsed = (now - self._last_alert[rule]).total_seconds() / 60
            if elapsed < self.cooldown:
                return
        self._last_alert[rule] = now
        send_slack_alert(f"Budget warning: ${cost:.2f} / ${budget:.2f}")
```

### Anti-Pattern 3: "Character Count as Token Proxy"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — off by 25-40% depending on content
input_tokens = len(prompt)  # characters, NOT tokens
cost = (input_tokens / 1000) * 0.005
# A 10,000-char prompt is ~2,500 tokens, not 10,000
# This overestimates cost by 4x!
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — use API response headers or tokenizer
# Option A: API response headers (exact)
response = client.chat.completions.create(...)
input_tokens = response.usage.prompt_tokens
output_tokens = response.usage.completion_tokens

# Option B: Pre-call estimate with tokenizer
import tiktoken
encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4o")
input_tokens = len(encoder.encode(prompt))
```

### Anti-Pattern 4: "Ignore Error Costs"

```python
# ❌ WRONG — errors are free, right?
try:
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    record_cost(response.usage.prompt_tokens, response.usage.completion_tokens)
except Exception:
    pass  # no cost recorded — but API still charged for input tokens!
```

```python
# ✅ CORRECT — track wasted spend from failures
try:
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    record_cost(
        input_tokens=response.usage.prompt_tokens,
        output_tokens=response.usage.completion_tokens,
        status="success",
    )
except Exception as e:
    # API still charged for input processing
    estimated_input = len(encoder.encode(prompt))
    record_cost(
        input_tokens=estimated_input,
        output_tokens=0,
        status="error",
        error_type=type(e).__name__,
    )
```

---

## ⚪ 2.10.13 「 Performance Benchmark Table 」

| Operation | Overhead per LLM Call | Memory | Notes |
|-----------|----------------------:|-------:|-------|
| Cost event creation + JSON serialize | 0.02 ms | 0.5 KB | Negligible vs LLM latency |
| Prometheus counter increment (5 labels) | 0.005 ms | 0.1 KB | In-process, no I/O |
| Structured log emission (JSON) | 0.08 ms | 1 KB | File I/O buffered |
| Anomaly detection (z-score check) | 0.01 ms | 8 KB (window) | Pure math |
| Budget check (3 rules) | 0.003 ms | 0.2 KB | Simple comparisons |
| Slack webhook dispatch | 50-200 ms | 2 KB | Async, fire-and-forget |
| CloudWatch PutMetricData | 10-50 ms | 1 KB | Batch every 60s |
| Prometheus /metrics scrape (100 metrics) | 5 ms | 50 KB | Every 15s by Prometheus |
| Dashboard query (1h window, 5 panels) | 200-500 ms | 10 MB | Grafana → Prometheus |
| Daily report generation | 2-5 s | 50 MB | Aggregation over 24h |

**Key insight:** Total instrumentation overhead is **< 0.1ms per LLM call**. A typical LLM call takes 500-5000ms. Cost observability adds **< 0.02%** overhead — effectively free.

---

```
                                    │
                       ┌────────────▼────────────┐
                       │  Yes                    │  No
                       │  → CloudWatch           │  → Prometheus
                       │    + structured logs     │    + Grafana
                       │    + metric filters      │    (docker-compose)
                       └─────────────────────────┘
                                                
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
```

## 🟢 2.10.15 「 Migration Path 」

### Step 0 — Structured Cost Logging (Day 1)

```python
"""Add structured JSON cost logging to LLM call sites."""
import json
import time


def log_llm_cost(
    logger,
    endpoint: str,
    input_tokens: int,
    output_tokens: int,
    model: str,
    latency_ms: float,
    tenant_id: str = "",
    session_id: str = "",
    status: str = "success",
):
    pricing = {"gpt-4o": {"input": 0.005, "output": 0.015}}
    prices = pricing.get(model, {"input": 0.01, "output": 0.03})
    cost = (input_tokens / 1000) * prices["input"] + (output_tokens / 1000) * prices["output"]

    logger.info(json.dumps({
        "event": "llm_cost",
        "endpoint": endpoint,
        "model": model,
        "input_tokens": input_tokens,
        "output_tokens": output_tokens,
        "cost_usd": round(cost, 6),
        "latency_ms": round(latency_ms, 2),
        "tenant_id": tenant_id,
        "session_id": session_id,
        "status": status,
    }))
```

### Step 1 — Metrics Extraction (Day 2-3)

```python
"""Set up CloudWatch metric filter or Prometheus exporter."""

# Option A: CloudWatch (if using AWS)
# Create metric filter on log group:
#   Filter: { $.event = "llm_cost" }
#   Metric: LLMCostUSD = $.cost_usd

# Option B: Prometheus
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, start_http_server

llm_cost = Counter("llm_cost_usd", "LLM cost", ["endpoint", "tenant_id"])
llm_latency = Histogram("llm_latency_ms", "LLM latency", ["endpoint"])

start_http_server(9090)
```

### Step 2 — Basic Dashboard (Day 3-5)

```python
"""Create Grafana dashboard with core panels."""
CORE_PANELS = [
    "Daily total spend (stat)",
    "Cost by endpoint (pie chart)",
    "Cost trend 7d (time series)",
    "Top tenants (table)",
    "Error waste rate (stat)",
    "Budget gauge (gauge)",
]
# Import grafana_dashboard.json into Grafana
# Connect to Prometheus data source
```

### Step 3 — Alerts + Anomaly Detection (Week 2)

```python
"""Configure budget alerts and anomaly detection."""
# 1. Set daily budget alert in Grafana:
#    sum(increase(llm_cost_usd[24h])) > 200
#    → Slack notification

# 2. Set per-call anomaly alert:
#    histogram_quantile(0.99, llm_cost_per_call) > 1.0
#    → PagerDuty notification

# 3. Set weekly report:
#    Cron job generates summary email every Monday
```

---

## 💡 2.10.16 「 Interview / Viva Q&A 」

**Q1: What are the essential metrics to track for LLM cost observability?**

**A1:** Five core metrics: (1) **Input tokens** — prompt cost, the dominant component for long-context apps; (2) **Output tokens** — completion cost, typically 2-3x the per-token price of input; (3) **Latency** — correlated with cost (longer calls = more output tokens) and important for user experience; (4) **Error rate** — failed calls still incur input token costs ("wasted spend"); (5) **Call volume** — total calls per time window, essential for capacity planning and anomaly detection. Each metric should be sliced by dimensions: model, endpoint, tenant, and intent/template. The combination of these five metrics with four dimensions gives complete cost visibility.

**Q2: How do you attribute LLM costs to specific tenants in a multi-tenant application?**

**A2:** Attach `tenant_id` as a label/dimension on every LLM cost event at the point of instrumentation — inside the LLM service layer, not at the API endpoint. This captures costs from retries and background processing that the endpoint layer doesn't see. Aggregate by tenant using the metrics pipeline (Prometheus `sum by (tenant_id)` or CloudWatch dimension). For billing, generate per-tenant monthly reports from the time-series data. Important: validate tenant attribution accuracy by cross-checking against API-level request counts — if tenant A made 60% of requests, they should account for roughly 60% of costs (adjusted for intent mix).

**Q3: How do you prevent alert fatigue in a cost monitoring system?**

**A3:** Four strategies: (1) **Cooldown periods** — after firing an alert, suppress the same alert for 30-60 minutes; (2) **Severity tiers** — INFO (log only), WARNING (Slack), CRITICAL (PagerDuty), with different thresholds and channels; (3) **Rate-based alerts over absolute** — alert on cost-per-minute exceeding 2x the rolling average, not on absolute amounts that vary by time of day; (4) **Grouped alerts** — batch multiple violations into a single notification rather than one per tenant. Also: review and tune thresholds monthly based on actual traffic patterns. An alert that fires daily without action should be deleted or recalibrated.

**Q4: What's the difference between pre-call token estimation and post-call exact counting?**

**A4:** Pre-call estimation uses a tokenizer (tiktoken) on the prompt text before sending it to the API. It's useful for context window budget checking and approximate cost prediction, but it doesn't know the exact output token count or any server-side transformations. Post-call exact counting reads the `usage` field from the API response (OpenAI returns `prompt_tokens` and `completion_tokens`). Post-call is authoritative for billing but arrives too late for overflow prevention. Best practice: use pre-call estimation for budget guards (reject requests that would exceed context window) and post-call exact counts for cost recording and dashboards.

**Q5: How do you detect cost anomalies vs. legitimate traffic spikes?**

**A5:** Use z-score analysis on hourly cost over a sliding window (168 hours = 1 week gives day-of-week seasonality). A cost spike > 2.5 standard deviations from the mean triggers investigation. To distinguish anomalies from legitimate spikes: (1) Check call volume — if volume also spiked proportionally, it's traffic-driven (marketing campaign, new tenant onboarding); (2) Check cost-per-call — if volume is normal but cost-per-call spiked, it's a prompt change or model switch; (3) Check error rate — if errors spiked, it's wasted retries. The anomaly detector flags the spike; the dashboard dimensions reveal the cause.

---

## ✅ 2.10.17 「 Production Readiness Checklist 」

- [ ] Every LLM call site instrumented with token + cost logging
- [ ] Structured JSON log format with all attribution dimensions
- [ ] Metrics pipeline operational (Prometheus/CloudWatch/Datadog)
- [ ] Dashboard deployed with at least 6 core panels
- [ ] Per-endpoint cost attribution verified against billing
- [ ] Per-tenant cost attribution verified against request counts
- [ ] Daily budget alert configured and tested (Slack/email)
- [ ] Critical budget alert configured and tested (PagerDuty)
- [ ] Anomaly detection has minimum 24h of baseline data
- [ ] Error cost ("waste") tracked separately from successful calls
- [ ] Weekly cost report automated and distributed to stakeholders
- [ ] Dashboard access granted to engineering, ops, and finance teams

---

## 🟡 2.10.18 「 Troubleshooting Guide 」

| Symptom | Check | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Dashboard shows zero cost | Verify structured logs are being emitted | Add `logger.info(json.dumps({...}))` to LLM call sites |
| Token counts don't match billing | API response `usage` field not captured | Use `response.usage.prompt_tokens` instead of estimation |
| Cost per tenant doesn't sum to total | Missing tenant_id on some calls | Add tenant_id propagation through all code paths |
| Alerts firing constantly | Thresholds set too low | Calibrate against 2 weeks of actual traffic data |
| No alerts firing at all | Webhook/channel misconfigured | Test alert dispatch with a manual trigger |
| Prometheus cardinality explosion | High-cardinality labels (session_id) | Use session_id only in logs, not metrics labels |
| Dashboard slow to load | Too many time-series queries | Reduce label cardinality; use recording rules |
| Anomaly detection false positives | Insufficient baseline data | Wait for 7+ days of data before enabling |
| Cost drift between environments | Different models/prompts per env | Track metrics per-environment with env label |
| Monthly report totals differ from bill | Missing call sites or retry costs | Audit all LLM call sites; include retry token costs |

---

## 💰 2.10.19 「 Cost Calculator 」

### Dashboard Infrastructure Cost

| Component | Self-Hosted Cost | Managed/SaaS Cost | Notes |
|-----------|----------------:|------------------:|-------|
| Prometheus | $0 (software) + $30-100/mo (server) | — | 1 vCPU, 4GB RAM sufficient for < 100K metrics |
| Grafana | $0 (OSS) + server above | $29-299/mo (Grafana Cloud) | OSS is feature-complete |
| CloudWatch | — | $0.30/metric/mo + $0.01/1K API calls | ~$15-50/mo for typical setup |
| Datadog APM | — | $36/host/mo + $0.10/GB logs | Enterprise-grade, expensive |
| AlertManager | $0 (with Prometheus) | Included in managed | PagerDuty: $21/user/mo extra |
| Storage (90d retention) | $5-20/mo (SSD) | Included in managed | ~1GB/month for 10K calls/day |

### ROI Calculation

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Dashboard infrastructure cost | ~$50-100/month (self-hosted) |
| Setup time (engineering) | 3-5 days (one-time) |
| Maintenance time | 2-4 hours/month |
| Typical optimization found | 15-25% cost reduction |
| Monthly LLM spend (baseline) | $5,000 |
| Monthly savings from optimization | $750 - $1,250 |
| **Net monthly benefit** | **$650 - $1,150** |
| **Payback period** | **< 1 month** |

### Cost of NOT Having Observability

| Scenario | Hidden Cost | Frequency |
|----------|------------|-----------|
| Undetected prompt regression (+30% tokens) | $1,500/month | 2-3x/year |
| Retry storm (3x normal cost for 4 hours) | $200/incident | 1-2x/month |
| Tenant abuse (automated bot hitting API) | $500+/incident | 1-2x/year |
| Model misconfiguration (GPT-4 instead of GPT-4o-mini) | $2,000/month | Until noticed |
| Unused feature still burning tokens | $300/month | Indefinite |
| **Total hidden cost without observability** | **$2,000-5,000/year** | |

**Key insight:** At 100K calls/month, the cost observability dashboard typically identifies **$750-3,750/month in savings** from optimizations it surfaces (prompt compression for the top-3 templates, caching for repeated queries, model routing for simple intents). The monitoring infrastructure costs **$80/month** — a **9x-47x return**. The dashboard doesn't save money directly; it tells you exactly where to point the optimization techniques from Chapters 2.1-2.9 for maximum impact.

# 📊 Part 2 — Comparison Matrix: Token & Prompt Reduction Techniques

> Every chapter in Part 2 introduced a technique for reducing token usage, managing costs, or optimizing prompt efficiency. This matrix provides a **single-page comparison** across all ten techniques so you can quickly decide which ones to adopt, in what order, and at what scale.

---

## Matrix 1: Technique Overview

| # | Technique | Core Idea | Typical Token Savings | Implementation Effort | Risk Level |
|---|-----------|-----------|----------------------|----------------------|------------|
| 2.1 | Prompt Compression | Algorithmically shorten prompts while preserving meaning | 30–60% | Medium | Medium — lossy compression may drop critical tokens |
| 2.2 | Semantic Caching | Cache LLM responses keyed by semantic similarity | 40–80% (on cache hit) | High | Low — cache miss = normal call |
| 2.3 | Context Window Management | Strategically fill/trim context to stay within limits | 20–40% | Medium | Medium — aggressive trimming loses context |
| 2.4 | Message History Compaction | Summarize or prune old conversation turns | 50–70% | Low–Medium | Medium — summary may lose details |
| 2.5 | Structured Output | Use schemas/enums to constrain output tokens | 10–30% | Low | Low — schema validation catches drift |
| 2.6 | Model Routing | Route simple queries to cheaper/smaller models | 30–70% cost savings | High | Medium — misrouting degrades quality |
| 2.7 | Batching & Parallelism | Combine multiple calls into fewer, larger calls | 15–25% overhead reduction | Medium | Low — bounded by rate limits |
| 2.8 | Token-Aware Chunking | Split documents at token boundaries, not character | 10–20% fewer wasted tokens | Low | Low — deterministic splitting |
| 2.9 | Prompt Tokenometry | Measure and budget token costs of prompt templates | 5–15% via template optimization | Low | Very Low — measurement only |
| 2.10 | Cost Dashboards | Observe, alert, and attribute LLM spend | 0% direct savings; enables all others | Medium–High | Very Low — read-only observability |

---

```
║  Ch 2.5: Structured Output     ← Quick win, low risk          ║
║  Ch 2.8: Token-Aware Chunking  ← Fix ingestion pipeline       ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
                            │
                            ▼
Stage 2: Quick Wins (Week 3-4)
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  Ch 2.4: Message Compaction    ← Biggest bang for buck         ║
║  Ch 2.3: Context Window Mgmt  ← Prevent context overflow      ║
║  Ch 2.1: Prompt Compression   ← Reduce input token costs      ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
                            │
                            ▼

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
```

---

## Matrix 3: Compatibility and Synergies

| Technique Pair | Synergy | Notes |
|---------------|---------|-------|
| Prompt Compression + Tokenometry | 🟢 Strong | Tokenometry identifies bloated templates; compression shrinks them |
| Semantic Caching + Model Routing | 🟢 Strong | Cache handles repeated queries; routing handles novel ones cheaply |
| Context Window + Message Compaction | 🟢 Strong | Compaction reduces history; window management fills freed space with docs |
| Structured Output + Batching | 🟢 Strong | Structured responses parse reliably in batch pipelines |
| Token-Aware Chunking + Prompt Compression | 🟡 Moderate | Better chunks = less wasted context; compression reduces further |
| Cost Dashboard + All Others | 🟢 Strong | Dashboard measures impact of every other technique |
| Semantic Caching + Message Compaction | 🟡 Moderate | Compacted history changes embedding — cache hit rate may drop |
| Prompt Compression + Structured Output | 🔴 Conflict | Compression may corrupt schema instructions in prompts |
| Model Routing + Prompt Compression | 🟡 Moderate | Compressed prompts may confuse smaller models |
| Batching + Model Routing | 🔴 Conflict | Batched calls go to one model; routing wants per-query model selection |

---

## Matrix 4: Cost Impact at Scale

| Technique | Monthly Cost @ 10K calls | Monthly Cost @ 100K calls | Savings vs. Baseline | Cumulative Savings |
|-----------|-------------------------|--------------------------|---------------------|--------------------|
| **Baseline (no optimization)** | $400 | $4,000 | — | — |
| + Structured Output (Ch 2.5) | $360 | $3,600 | 10% | 10% |
| + Message Compaction (Ch 2.4) | $252 | $2,520 | 30% | 37% |
| + Prompt Compression (Ch 2.1) | $202 | $2,016 | 20% | 50% |
| + Context Window Mgmt (Ch 2.3) | $181 | $1,814 | 10% | 55% |
| + Semantic Caching (Ch 2.2) | $127 | $1,270 | 30% cache hit | 68% |
| + Model Routing (Ch 2.6) | $89 | $889 | 30% cost routing | 78% |
| + Tokenometry + Chunking (Ch 2.8-19) | $80 | $800 | 10% | 80% |
| **Fully Optimized** | **$80** | **$800** | — | **80%** |

> **Key Insight:** The first three techniques (structured output, message compaction, prompt compression) deliver 50% savings with low-to-medium effort. The remaining techniques add another 30% but require significantly more infrastructure.

## Matrix 5: Technology Stack

| Technique | Primary Libraries | Infrastructure Needed | Cloud Services |
|-----------|------------------|----------------------|---------------|
| Prompt Compression | LLMLingua, LongLLMLingua, gzip | GPU (for LLMLingua) or CPU | — |
| Semantic Caching | GPTCache, Redis, FAISS | Redis cluster, embedding model | ElastiCache, MemoryDB |
| Context Window Mgmt | tiktoken, transformers | — | — |
| Message Compaction | langmem, LangGraph | Checkpointer (DynamoDB/Postgres) | DynamoDB |
| Structured Output | Pydantic, instructor, outlines | — | — |

| Token-Aware Chunking | tiktoken, RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter | — | — |
| Prompt Tokenometry | tiktoken, PyYAML | CI pipeline | — |
| Cost Dashboard | Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch | Monitoring stack | CloudWatch, Grafana Cloud |
