```
                                  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
                                  │ Ch 2.2:       │  │ Ch 2.6:       │
                                  │ Semantic     │  │ Model        │
                                  │ Caching      │  │ Routing      │
                                  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘
```

---

## Matrix 7: Risk and Mitigation

| Technique | Primary Risk | Mitigation | Rollback Strategy |
|-----------|-------------|------------|-------------------|
| Prompt Compression | Lossy compression drops critical context | A/B test compressed vs. original; set minimum quality threshold | Feature flag to bypass compression |
| Semantic Caching | Stale cache returns outdated answers | TTL-based eviction; cache invalidation on data changes | Cache bypass header for debugging |
| Context Window Mgmt | Aggressive trimming causes hallucination | Keep minimum context floor; monitor answer quality metrics | Increase window allocation gradually |
| Message Compaction | Summary loses important conversation details | Preserve recent N messages uncompacted; entity extraction | Increase summary token budget |
| Structured Output | Schema too rigid for nuanced responses | Hybrid: structured metadata + free-form explanation field | Fall back to unstructured output |
| Model Routing | Small model produces low-quality response | Confidence threshold routing; automatic escalation | Route all to large model (cost increase) |
| Batching | Batch timeout adds latency for fast queries | Adaptive batch window; priority queue for interactive calls | Disable batching, process individually |
| Token-Aware Chunking | Chunk boundaries split semantic units | Overlap buffer; paragraph-aware splitting | Revert to character-based splitting |
| Prompt Tokenometry | Token budget too tight for new features | Budget slack (10-15% reserve); quarterly budget review | Remove budget CI gate temporarily |
| Cost Dashboard | Alert fatigue from noisy thresholds | Anomaly detection (z-score) instead of fixed thresholds; cooldown periods | Mute non-critical alerts |

---
---

# 📝 Part 2 Summary — Token & Prompt Reduction

> *"The cheapest token is the one you never send."*

---

## What We Covered

Part 2 explored **ten techniques** for reducing token usage, managing costs, and optimizing prompt efficiency in LLM-powered applications. Unlike Part 1's focus on graph orchestration mechanics, Part 2 addressed the **economic reality** of running LLMs in production: every token costs money, every millisecond costs patience, and every unnecessary call costs both.

---

## The Three Pillars of Token Optimization

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                   TOKEN OPTIMIZATION PILLARS                        ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                                                      ║
║   Pillar 1: REDUCE              Pillar 2: REUSE                     ║
║   ┌──────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────┐                ║
║   │ Ch 2.1: Compress  │          │ Ch 2.2: Cache     │                ║
║   │ Ch 2.3: Trim      │          │ Ch 2.7: Batch     │                ║
║   │ Ch 2.4: Compact   │          │ Ch 2.8: Chunk     │                ║
║   │ Ch 2.5: Structure │          │ Ch 2.9: Measure   │                ║
║   └──────────────────┘          └──────────────────┘                ║
║                                                                      ║
║   Pillar 3: ROUTE                                                    ║
║   ┌──────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────┐                ║
║   │ Ch 2.6: Route     │          │ Ch 2.10: Observe   │                ║
║   │ (right model for │          │ (measure to      │                ║
║   │  right task)     │          │  improve)        │                ║
║   └──────────────────┘          └──────────────────┘                ║
║                                                                      ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

---

## Key Insights by Chapter

| Chapter | One-Line Takeaway |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Ch 2.1: Prompt Compression** | LLMLingua and extractive methods can cut prompt tokens 40-60% with <5% quality loss when calibrated |
| **Ch 2.2: Semantic Caching** | Embedding-based cache keys turn repeated-intent queries into sub-10ms responses at near-zero marginal cost |
| **Ch 2.3: Context Window Management** | Treating the context window as a scarce resource with explicit budgets prevents silent truncation disasters |
| **Ch 2.4: Message Compaction** | Rolling summaries with langmem keep 20+ turn conversations in ~500 tokens while preserving key facts |
| **Ch 2.5: Structured Output** | Pydantic schemas on output save 10-30% tokens AND eliminate parsing failures — double win |
| **Ch 2.6: Model Routing** | Routing 60% of queries to GPT-3.5/Haiku cuts costs 40-70% when your quality classifier is accurate |
| **Ch 2.7: Batching** | Combining N independent calls into one batch reduces per-call overhead and unlocks bulk pricing tiers |
| **Ch 2.8: Token-Aware Chunking** | Splitting on token boundaries instead of characters eliminates wasted partial-token overhead in embeddings |
| **Ch 2.9: Prompt Tokenometry** | You cannot optimize what you do not measure — token budgets in CI catch prompt bloat before production |
| **Ch 2.10: Cost Dashboards** | Prometheus + Grafana dashboards with per-intent attribution turn "LLM costs are high" into actionable line items |

---

## The Optimization Sequence

Based on the effort-to-impact ratios from Part 2, the recommended implementation order:

```
Impact
  ▲
  │
  │   ★ Message Compaction (Ch 2.4)
  │          ★ Model Routing (Ch 2.6)
  │     ★ Semantic Caching (Ch 2.2)
  │          ★ Prompt Compression (Ch 2.1)
  │     ★ Context Window Mgmt (Ch 2.3)
  │                    ★ Structured Output (Ch 2.5)
  │          ★ Cost Dashboard (Ch 2.10)
  │     ★ Batching (Ch 2.7)
  │          ★ Token-Aware Chunking (Ch 2.8)
  │               ★ Prompt Tokenometry (Ch 2.9)
  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────► Effort
       Low                                    High
```

**Start bottom-left, work toward top-left.** Tokenometry and chunking cost almost nothing to implement but establish measurement foundations. Message compaction and prompt compression deliver the biggest savings for moderate effort. Model routing and semantic caching deliver massive savings but require infrastructure investment.

---

## Cost Savings Summary

For a production system processing **100,000 LLM calls/month** at an average cost of $0.04/call ($4,000/month baseline):

| Optimization Layer | Monthly Savings | Cumulative Cost | Cumulative Reduction |
|-------------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------------|
| No optimization | $0 | $4,000 | 0% |
| Structured Output + Chunking | $500 | $3,500 | 12.5% |
| Message Compaction + Window Mgmt | $1,200 | $2,300 | 42.5% |
| Prompt Compression | $460 | $1,840 | 54% |
| Semantic Caching (30% hit rate) | $550 | $1,290 | 67.5% |
| Model Routing (40% to small model) | $400 | $890 | 77.5% |
| **Total Annual Savings** | **$37,320/year** | **$890/mo** | **77.5%** |

> **The 80/20 rule applies:** Message compaction + prompt compression + semantic caching deliver ~60% of total savings. Everything else is optimization on the margin.

---

## Connecting Part 1 and Part 2

Part 1 taught you how to **build** LLM-powered graph applications. Part 2 taught you how to **afford** them at scale.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    THE COMPLETE PICTURE                          │
│                                                                  │
│   Part 1: Build It Right          Part 2: Run It Cheap          │
│   ┌─────────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────┐      │
│   │ HITL Patterns       │────────▶│ Prompt Compression   │      │
│   │ Graph Persistence   │────────▶│ Semantic Caching     │      │
│   │ Dynamic Graphs      │────────▶│ Context Window Mgmt  │      │
│   │ Multi-Agent         │────────▶│ Message Compaction   │      │
│   │ Streaming           │────────▶│ Structured Output    │      │
│   │ Error Recovery      │────────▶│ Model Routing        │      │
│   │ Observability       │────────▶│ Batching             │      │
│   │ Subgraphs           │────────▶│ Token-Aware Chunking │      │
│   │ Map-Reduce          │────────▶│ Prompt Tokenometry   │      │
│   │ Alternatives        │────────▶│ Cost Dashboards      │      │
│   └─────────────────────┘         └─────────────────────┘      │
│                                                                  │
│   "A graph that works"     →      "A graph that scales"         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Final Thought

Token optimization is not a one-time project — it is an **ongoing practice**. Models change pricing, new compression techniques emerge, usage patterns shift. The techniques in Part 2 give you a toolkit, but the real advantage comes from building the **muscle of measurement**: instrument first, optimize second, verify always.

The dashboard (Ch 2.10) is not the last chapter by accident. It is the chapter that makes every other chapter's savings **visible and accountable**. Without measurement, optimization is guesswork. With measurement, it is engineering.

---
---



# 📖「 Chapter 0 」— Glossary & Index

> <span style="color:#00D4FF">**Reference**</span> · ~100 terms · Alphabetical

---

This glossary defines every key term used across both parts of the minibook. Each entry includes: the term, a concise definition, and the chapter(s) where it is discussed in depth. Use this as a quick-reference lookup while reading or implementing.

---

## A

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **A/B Testing (LLM)** | Running two prompt/model variants simultaneously to compare output quality and cost | Ch 2.1, Ch 2.6 |
| **add_messages Reducer** | LangGraph built-in reducer that appends new messages to state and processes `RemoveMessage` objects for deletion | Ch 1.2, Ch 2.4 |
| **Agent Orchestration** | Coordinating multiple LLM-powered agents to collaboratively solve complex tasks | Ch 1.4, Ch 1.10 |
| **Anomaly Detection (Cost)** | Statistical methods (z-score, IQR) to detect unusual spikes in LLM spending | Ch 2.10 |
| **Async Streaming** | Non-blocking token-by-token delivery of LLM responses using Python's `async for` | Ch 1.5 |

---

## B

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Backoff (Exponential)** | Retry strategy that doubles wait time between attempts (e.g., 1s → 2s → 4s) to avoid overwhelming services | Ch 1.6 |
| **Batch Window** | Time period during which individual LLM calls are collected before being sent as a single batch request | Ch 2.7 |
| **Box-Drawing Characters** | Unicode characters (`╔═╗║╚═╝┌─┐│└─┘`) used for ASCII diagrams in documentation | All |
| **Breakpoint** | A named pause point in a LangGraph graph where execution halts for human review before continuing | Ch 1.1 |
| **Budget Gate (CI)** | A continuous integration check that fails the build if prompt template token counts exceed defined thresholds | Ch 2.9 |

---

## C

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Cache Hit Rate** | Percentage of queries that find a matching response in the semantic cache, avoiding an LLM call | Ch 2.2 |
| **Checkpoint** | A serialized snapshot of graph state at a specific point in execution, enabling resume and time-travel | Ch 1.2 |
| **Checkpointer** | LangGraph persistence backend (e.g., `MemorySaver`, `DynamoDBSaver`) that stores and retrieves checkpoints | Ch 1.2, Ch 1.8 |
| **Circuit Breaker** | Pattern that stops calling a failing service after N consecutive failures, allowing recovery time | Ch 1.6 |
| **ClassifyIntentNode** | DC-Copilot node that uses FastText to classify user input into one of 10 intent types | Ch 1.3, Ch 1.6 |
| **Command Pattern** | LangGraph pattern using `Command(goto=..., update=...)` for dynamic routing with state updates | Ch 1.3 |
| **Compression Ratio** | The fraction of original tokens remaining after prompt compression (e.g., 0.4 = 60% reduction) | Ch 2.1 |
| **Conditional Edge** | LangGraph edge that routes to different nodes based on a function evaluating current state | Ch 1.1, Ch 1.3 |
| **ConfigurableField** | LangGraph mechanism for injecting runtime parameters via `RunnableConfig["configurable"]` | Ch 1.3, Ch 1.8 |
| **Context Preloading** | Pre-computing expensive context data (Snowflake queries) during session initialization rather than at query time | Ch 1.9, Ch 2.3 |
| **Context Window** | Maximum number of tokens an LLM can process in a single call (input + output combined) | Ch 2.3 |
| **CopilotState** | Pydantic model defining DC-Copilot's graph state with typed fields and LangGraph reducers | Ch 1.2, Ch 2.5 |
| **Cost Attribution** | Tagging LLM costs with metadata (intent, endpoint, tenant) to identify spending by category | Ch 2.10 |
| **CrewAI** | Multi-agent framework using role-based agent definitions with built-in task delegation | Ch 1.10 |

---

## D

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)** | Queue that stores messages that failed processing after maximum retries, for later investigation | Ch 1.6 |
| **Decision Flowchart** | ASCII box-drawing diagram showing if/then routing logic for choosing between approaches | All (section N.14) |
| **Deferred Checkpointer** | Checkpoint backend that writes asynchronously to avoid blocking the streaming response path | Ch 1.2 |
| **Dependency Injection** | Passing services (LLM, DB clients) via `RunnableConfig` rather than hardcoding in nodes | Ch 1.3, Ch 1.8 |
| **Dynamic Graph** | A LangGraph graph whose nodes and edges are determined at runtime based on input or configuration | Ch 1.3 |

---

## E

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Embedding** | Dense vector representation of text (typically 1536-dim for OpenAI) used for semantic similarity | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |
| **Entity Extraction** | Identifying and tracking named entities (asset IDs, work order numbers) across conversation turns | Ch 2.4 |
| **Error Recovery** | Patterns for handling failures gracefully: retry, fallback, circuit breaker, dead letter queue | Ch 1.6 |
| **Event Streaming** | Server-Sent Events (SSE) protocol for pushing LLM tokens to clients as they are generated | Ch 1.5 |
| **Exponential Backoff** | See *Backoff (Exponential)* | Ch 1.6 |

---

## F

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **FAISS** | Facebook AI Similarity Search — library for efficient similarity search on dense vectors | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |
| **Fallback Response** | Pre-defined safe response returned when all retry attempts are exhausted | Ch 1.6 |
| **FALLBACK_PHRASES** | List of phrases indicating non-answers (e.g., "not available in provided documents") that trigger retry | Ch 1.6 |
| **Fan-out / Fan-in** | Pattern where a single node dispatches work to multiple parallel nodes, then collects results | Ch 1.9 |
| **FastText** | Facebook's lightweight text classification library used for intent classification | Ch 1.3 |
| **Feature Flag** | Runtime toggle to enable/disable features without deployment, useful for gradual rollout | Ch 2.1, Ch 2.6 |
| **Few-Shot Prompting** | Including example input-output pairs in the prompt to guide LLM behavior | Ch 2.1, Ch 2.9 |

---

## G

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **GPTCache** | Open-source semantic caching library for LLM responses with pluggable similarity backends | Ch 2.2 |
| **Graceful Degradation** | System continues operating with reduced functionality when a component fails | Ch 1.6 |
| **Grafana** | Open-source visualization platform for building dashboards from time-series metrics | Ch 1.7, Ch 2.10 |
| **Graph Persistence** | Saving and restoring LangGraph execution state across sessions using checkpointers | Ch 1.2 |

---

## H

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **HITL (Human-in-the-Loop)** | Pattern where graph execution pauses for human approval before proceeding | Ch 1.1 |
| **HNSW** | Hierarchical Navigable Small World — approximate nearest neighbor algorithm used in vector stores | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |
| **Hybrid Search** | Combining vector similarity search with keyword (BM25) search for better retrieval | Ch 2.8 |

---

## I

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Idempotency** | Property where repeating an operation produces the same result (critical for retry safety) | Ch 1.6, Ch 2.7 |
| **Intent Classification** | Categorizing user input into predefined intent types to route processing | Ch 1.3, Ch 2.6 |
| **IntentFanoutNode** | DC-Copilot node that routes to intent-specific prompt builders based on classified intent | Ch 1.3, Ch 1.9 |
| **IntentType** | Enum defining DC-Copilot's 10 intent categories (GREETINGS, DIAGNOSIS, FIX, etc.) | Ch 2.5 |
| **Interrupt** | LangGraph mechanism (`interrupt()`) that pauses graph execution and returns control to the caller | Ch 1.1 |

---

## J–K

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Jitter** | Random delay added to retry timing to prevent thundering herd when multiple clients retry simultaneously | Ch 1.6 |
| **JSON Mode** | LLM output mode that constrains responses to valid JSON, reducing parsing failures | Ch 2.5 |
| **KNN Vector** | K-nearest neighbors search on vector embeddings for finding semantically similar documents | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |

---

## L

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **LangGraph** | LangChain's framework for building stateful, multi-step LLM applications as directed graphs | All |
| **LangSmith** | LangChain's observability platform for tracing, evaluating, and monitoring LLM applications | Ch 1.7 |
| **langmem** | LangChain memory library providing `SummarizationNode` for automatic conversation summarization | Ch 2.4 |
| **LiteLLM** | Unified API wrapper supporting 100+ LLM providers with consistent interface | Ch 2.6 |
| **LLMLingua** | Microsoft Research library for prompt compression using small language models as token classifiers | Ch 2.1 |
| **LLMService** | DC-Copilot service wrapping Azure OpenAI with retry logic, quality validation, and streaming | Ch 1.6, Ch 2.1 |
| **LRU Cache** | Least Recently Used cache eviction strategy; used in DC-Copilot for template loading (`@lru_cache`) | Ch 2.9 |

---

## M

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Map-Reduce** | Pattern where work is split across parallel workers (map), then results are combined (reduce) | Ch 1.9 |
| **MAX_PROMPT_LENGTH** | DC-Copilot constant (320,000 chars) — prompts exceeding this are truncated before LLM call | Ch 2.1, Ch 2.3 |
| **MemorySaver** | LangGraph's in-memory checkpointer for development/testing (not for production) | Ch 1.2 |
| **Message Compaction** | Replacing older conversation messages with a condensed summary to reduce token usage | Ch 2.4 |
| **MIN_RESPONSE_LENGTH** | DC-Copilot constant (3 chars) — responses shorter than this trigger a retry | Ch 1.6 |
| **Model Cascade** | Sequential routing pattern: try cheapest model first, escalate to expensive model if quality is low | Ch 2.6 |
| **Model Routing** | Directing queries to different LLM models based on complexity, intent, or cost constraints | Ch 2.6 |
| **Multi-Agent** | Architecture using multiple specialized LLM agents that collaborate on complex tasks | Ch 1.4 |

---

## N–O

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Node (LangGraph)** | A function or callable in a LangGraph graph that receives state and returns state updates | All |
| **OpenSearch Serverless** | AWS managed vector database service using FAISS HNSW indices with IAM authentication | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |
| **Observability** | Ability to understand system behavior through logs, metrics, and traces | Ch 1.7, Ch 2.10 |
| **Overlap (Chunking)** | Number of characters/tokens shared between adjacent chunks to preserve context at boundaries | Ch 2.8 |

---

## P

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Parallel Execution** | Running multiple graph branches simultaneously using LangGraph's `Send()` or parallel nodes | Ch 1.5, Ch 1.9 |
| **Prometheus** | Open-source time-series metrics database with pull-based collection model | Ch 2.10 |
| **Prompt Compression** | Algorithmically shortening prompts while preserving semantic meaning to reduce token costs | Ch 2.1 |
| **Prompt Template** | YAML/text file with variable placeholders that gets populated at runtime to form the LLM prompt | Ch 2.9 |
| **Prompt Tokenometry** | Practice of measuring, budgeting, and tracking token costs of prompt templates over time | Ch 2.9 |
| **Pydantic** | Python data validation library using type annotations; used for graph state and structured output | Ch 2.5 |

---

## Q–R

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Quality Validation** | Checking LLM responses against criteria (length, fallback phrases, structure) before accepting | Ch 1.6 |
| **Rate Limiting** | Controlling request frequency to stay within API provider limits (tokens/min, requests/min) | Ch 2.7 |
| **RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter** | LangChain text splitter that recursively splits on separators (paragraph → sentence → word) | Ch 2.8 |
| **Reducer (LangGraph)** | Function that merges new state values with existing state (e.g., `add_messages`, `_keep_nonempty_str`) | Ch 1.2, Ch 1.8 |
| **RemoveMessage** | LangGraph message type that signals the `add_messages` reducer to delete a specific message by ID | Ch 2.4 |
| **Retry Logic** | Pattern of re-attempting failed operations with configurable attempts, delays, and conditions | Ch 1.6 |
| **RunnableConfig** | LangChain/LangGraph configuration object carrying runtime dependencies and metadata through the graph | Ch 1.3, Ch 1.8 |
| **Running Summary** | Incrementally updated summary of conversation history maintained by langmem's SummarizationNode | Ch 2.4 |

---

## S

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Safe Default** | Pre-defined fallback value a node returns on error to keep the pipeline running | Ch 1.6 |
| **Semantic Cache** | Cache that matches queries by meaning (embedding similarity) rather than exact string match | Ch 2.2 |
| **Semantic Chunking** | Splitting documents at meaningful boundaries (paragraphs, sections) rather than fixed character counts | Ch 2.8 |
| **Send()** | LangGraph primitive for dynamically spawning parallel node executions at runtime | Ch 1.9 |
| **SSE (Server-Sent Events)** | HTTP protocol for server-to-client streaming; used for delivering LLM tokens in real time | Ch 1.5 |
| **StateGraph** | LangGraph's primary graph class that manages typed state through node execution and reducers | All |
| **Streaming** | Delivering LLM output token-by-token as it's generated, rather than waiting for the full response | Ch 1.5 |
| **Structured Output** | Constraining LLM responses to predefined schemas (JSON, Pydantic models) for reliable parsing | Ch 2.5 |
| **Subgraph** | A compiled LangGraph graph embedded as a single node within a parent graph | Ch 1.8 |
| **SUMMARY_MAX_TOKENS** | DC-Copilot constant (4,000) — maximum token count for conversation history before trimming | Ch 2.4 |
| **SummarizationNode** | langmem node that generates and maintains running summaries of conversation history | Ch 2.4 |
| **SummarizationSubgraphState** | Minimal state model for DC-Copilot's summarization subgraph, avoiding concurrent update errors | Ch 1.8, Ch 2.4 |

---

## T

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Tenacity** | Python library for configurable retry behavior with decorators (`@retry`) | Ch 1.6 |
| **Thread ID** | Unique identifier (typically session_id) used to scope checkpointed state to a conversation | Ch 1.2 |
| **ThreadPoolExecutor** | Python concurrent.futures executor for running I/O-bound tasks in parallel threads | Ch 1.9, Ch 2.7 |
| **tiktoken** | OpenAI's fast BPE tokenizer library for counting tokens offline without API calls | Ch 2.3, Ch 2.8, Ch 2.9 |
| **Time-Travel Debugging** | Replaying graph execution from a historical checkpoint to diagnose issues | Ch 1.2 |
| **Token** | Smallest unit of text processed by an LLM; roughly ¾ of a word for English text | Ch 2.1, Ch 2.3 |
| **Token Budget** | Maximum token allocation for a specific purpose (system prompt, history, context, output) | Ch 2.3, Ch 2.9 |
| **Token-Aware Chunking** | Splitting documents based on token count rather than character count for precise context filling | Ch 2.8 |
| **Tokenometry** | See *Prompt Tokenometry* | Ch 2.9 |
| **TTL (Time-to-Live)** | Duration after which a cached value expires and must be refreshed | Ch 2.2 |

---

## U–V

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Update Listener** | Callback receiving state updates as graph nodes execute (used for logging and monitoring) | Ch 1.7 |
| **Variable Expansion** | Replacing `${variable}` placeholders in prompt templates with actual values at runtime | Ch 2.9 |
| **Vector Store** | Database optimized for storing and searching dense vector embeddings (OpenSearch, Pinecone, FAISS) | Ch 2.2, Ch 2.8 |

---

## W–Z

| Term | Definition | Chapters |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Windowed History** | Keeping only the N most recent messages in context, discarding or summarizing older ones | Ch 2.3, Ch 2.4 |
| **Work Order** | DC-Copilot domain concept: a maintenance task with associated assets, history, and documentation | Ch 1.9, Ch 2.3 |
| **Worker Pool** | Collection of background processes that consume tasks from a queue (e.g., SQS workers) | Ch 2.7 |
| **Z-Score** | Statistical measure of how many standard deviations a value is from the mean; used for anomaly detection | Ch 2.10 |

---

## Notation Conventions Used in This Book

| Notation | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `P.C.X` | Part P, Chapter C, Section X | `1.6.14` = Part 1, Chapter 6, Section 14 |
| `「 Title 」` | Japanese corner brackets for section titles | `「 Error Recovery 」` |
| `Ch N` | Chapter reference | "See Ch 2.2 for caching" |
| `10K / 100K` | Scale shorthand (10,000 / 100,000) | "At 100K calls/month" |
| `→` | Leads to / results in | "Retry → Fallback → DLQ" |
| `$X.XX` | US dollar cost | "$0.04 per call" |
| `~` | Approximately | "~500 tokens" |
| `P95 / P99` | 95th / 99th percentile | "P95 latency: 2.3s" |
